41:   ##
42:   # Thread-safe monitors for different contexts
43:   @monitors = {require: Monitor.new, inherited: Monitor.new, registry: Monitor.new, mcp: Monitor.new}
44: 
45:   ##
46:   # Model registry
47:   @registry = {}
48: 
49:   ##
50:   # Requires an optional runtime dependency
51:   # @raise [LLM::DependencyError]
52:   #  When the dependency cannot be loaded
53:   def self.require(name)
54:     super
55:   rescue ::LoadError
56:     names = {"xchan" => "xchan.rb", "net/http/persistent" => "net-http-persistent"}
57:     name = names[name] || name
58:     raise LLM::LoadError,
59:       "#{name} is an optional runtime dependency but it does not appear to be installed. " \
60:       "Consider 'gem install #{name}', adding '#{name}' to your Gemfile or " \
61:       "opting out of the functionality provided by '#{name}'"
62:   end
63: 
64:   ##
65:   # @param [Symbol, LLM::Provider] llm
66:   #  The name of a provider, or an instance of LLM::Provider
67:   # @return [LLM::Object]
68:   def self.registry_for(llm)
69:     lock(:registry) do
70:       name = Symbol === llm ? llm : llm.name
71:       @registry[name] ||= Registry.for(name)
72:     end
73:   end
74: 
75:   module_function
76: 
77:   ##
78:   # Returns the JSON adapter used by the library
79:   # @return [Class]
80:   #  Returns a class that responds to `dump` and `load`
81:   def json
82:     @json ||= JSONAdapter::JSON
83:   end
84: 
85:   ##
86:   # Sets the JSON adapter used by the library
87:   # @note
88:   #  This should be set once from the main thread when your program starts.
89:   #  Defaults to {LLM::JSONAdapter::JSON LLM::JSONAdapter::JSON}.
90:   # @param [Class, String, Symbol] adapter
91:   #  A JSON adapter class or its name
92:   # @return [void]
93:   def json=(adapter)
94:     @json = case adapter.to_s
95:     when "JSON", "json" then JSONAdapter::JSON
96:     when "Oj", "oj" then JSONAdapter::Oj
97:     when "Yajl", "yajl" then JSONAdapter::Yajl
98:     else
99:       is_class = Class === adapter
100:       is_subclass = is_class && adapter.ancestors.include?(LLM::JSONAdapter)
101:       if is_subclass
102:         adapter
103:       else
104:         raise TypeError, "Adapter must be a subclass of LLM::JSONAdapter"
105:       end
106:     end
107:   end
108: 
109:   ##
110:   # @param (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
111:   # @return (see LLM::Anthropic#initialize)
112:   def anthropic(**)
113:     lock(:require) { require_relative "llm/providers/anthropic" unless defined?(LLM::Anthropic) }
114:     LLM::Anthropic.new(**)

185:   # @option stdio [Array<String>] :argv
186:   #  The command to run for the MCP process
187:   # @option stdio [Hash] :env
188:   #  The environment variables to set for the MCP process
189:   # @option stdio [String, nil] :cwd

199:   # @param [Hash, nil] http
200:   # @option http [String] :url
201:   #  The base URL of the A2A agent (e.g., "https://agent.example.com")
202:   # @option http [Hash<String, String>] :headers
203:   #  Extra HTTP headers (e.g., Authorization)
204:   # @option http [Integer, nil] :timeout
205:   #  Request timeout in seconds
206:   # @option http [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] :transport
207:   #  Optional transport override
208:   # @param [Symbol] binding
209:   #  The protocol binding to use. One of `:rest` or `:jsonrpc`
210:   # @return [LLM::A2A]
211:   def a2a(http:, binding: :rest)
212:     LLM::A2A.http(**http, binding:)
213:   end

215:   ##
216:   # Define a function
217:   # @example
218:   #   LLM.function(:system) do |fn|
219:   #     fn.description "Run system command"
220:   #     fn.params do |schema|
221:   #       schema.object(command: schema.string.required)
222:   #     end
223:   #     fn.define do |command:|
224:   #       system(command)
225:   #     end
226:   #   end
227:   # @param [Symbol] key The function name / key
228:   # @param [Proc] b The block to define the function
229:   # @return [LLM::Function] The function object
230:   def function(key, &b)
231:     LLM::Function.new(key, &b)
232:   end

236:   # @param [Symbol] name The name of the lock
237:   # @param [Proc] & The block to execute within the lock

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # The Provider class represents an abstract class for
5: # LLM (Language Model) providers.
6: #
7: # @abstract
8: class LLM::Provider
9:   include LLM::Transport::Execution
10: 
11:   ##
12:   # @param [String, nil] key
13:   #  The secret key for authentication
14:   # @param [String] host
15:   #  The host address of the LLM provider
16:   # @param [Integer] port
17:   #  The port number
18:   # @param [Integer] timeout
19:   #  The number of seconds to wait for a response
20:   # @param [Boolean] ssl
21:   #  Whether to use SSL for the connection
22:   # @param [String] base_path
23:   #  Optional base path prefix for HTTP API routes.
24:   # @param [Boolean] persistent
25:   #  Whether to use a persistent connection.
26:   #  Requires the net-http-persistent gem.
27:   # @param [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] transport
28:   #  Optional override with any {LLM::Transport} instance or subclass.
29:   def initialize(key:, host:, port: 443, timeout: 60, ssl: true, base_path: "", persistent: false, transport: nil)
30:     @key = key
31:     @host = host
32:     @port = port
33:     @timeout = timeout
34:     @ssl = ssl
35:     @base_path = LLM::Utils.normalize_base_path(base_path)
36:     @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
37:     @headers = {"User-Agent" => "llm.rb v#{LLM::VERSION}"}
38:     @transport = LLM::Transport::Utils.resolve_transport(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:, transport:, persistent:)
39:     @monitor = Monitor.new
40:   end
41: 
42:   ##
43:   # Returns an inspection of the provider object
44:   # @return [String]
45:   # @note The secret key is redacted in inspect for security reasons
46:   def inspect
47:     "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @key=[REDACTED] @transport=#{transport.inspect} @tracer=#{tracer.inspect}>"
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
52:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
53:   # @return [Symbol]
54:   #  Returns the provider's name
55:   def name
56:     raise NotImplementedError
57:   end
58: 
59:   ##
60:   # Provides an embedding
61:   # @param [String, Array<String>] input
62:   #  The input to embed
63:   # @param [String] model
64:   #  The embedding model to use
65:   # @param [Hash] params
66:   #  Other embedding parameters
67:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
68:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
69:   # @return [LLM::Response]
70:   def embed(input, model: nil, **params)
71:     raise NotImplementedError
72:   end
73: 
74:   ##
75:   # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
76:   # @example
77:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
78:   #   messages = [{role: "system", content: "Your task is to answer all of my questions"}]
79:   #   res = llm.complete("5 + 2 ?", messages:)
80:   #   print "[#{res.messages[0].role}]", res.messages[0].content, "\n"
81:   # @param [String] prompt
82:   #  The input prompt to be completed
83:   # @param [Hash] params
84:   #  The parameters to maintain throughout the conversation.
85:   #  Any parameter the provider supports can be included and
86:   #  not only those listed here.
87:   # @option params [Symbol] :role Defaults to the provider's default role
88:   # @option params [String] :model Defaults to the provider's default model

280:   # This tracer is shared by the provider instance and becomes the fallback
281:   # whenever no scoped override is active.
282:   # @example
283:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
284:   #   llm.tracer = LLM::Tracer::Logger.new(llm, path: "/path/to/log.txt")
285:   # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
286:   #  A tracer
287:   # @return [void]
288:   def tracer=(tracer)
289:     @tracer = tracer
290:   end
291: 
292:   ##
293:   # Override the tracer for the current fiber while the block runs.
294:   # This is useful when you want per-request or per-turn tracing without
295:   # replacing the provider's default tracer.
296:   # @example
297:   #   llm.with_tracer(LLM::Tracer::Logger.new(llm, io: $stdout)) do
298:   #     llm.complete("hello", model: "gpt-5.4-mini")
299:   #   end
300:   # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
301:   # @yield
302:   # @return [Object]
303:   def with_tracer(tracer)
304:     had_override = weakmap.key?(self)
305:     previous = weakmap[self]
306:     weakmap[self] = tracer
307:     yield
308:   ensure
309:     if had_override
310:       weakmap[self] = previous
311:     elsif weakmap.respond_to?(:delete)
312:       weakmap.delete(self)
313:     else
314:       weakmap[self] = nil
315:     end
316:   end
317: 
318:   ##
319:   # Interrupt the active request, if any.
320:   # @param [Fiber] owner
321:   # @return [nil]
322:   def interrupt!(owner)
323:     transport.interrupt!(owner)
324:   end
325:   alias_method :cancel!, :interrupt!

329:   # @return [Object]
330:   # @api private
331:   def request_owner
332:     transport.request_owner
333:   end

338:   def streamable?(stream)

340:   end

345:   def key?

347:   end

352:     return suffix if @base_path.empty?
353:     "#{@base_path}#{suffix}"
354:   end
355: 
356:   attr_reader :base_uri, :host, :port, :timeout, :ssl, :transport
357:

359:   # The headers to include with a request
360:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
361:   #  (see LLM::Provider#complete)
362:   def headers
363:     raise NotImplementedError
364:   end

368:   #  Returns the class responsible for handling an unsuccessful LLM response
369:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
370:   #  (see LLM::Provider#complete)

373:   end

379:   end

384:   def stream_parser
385:     raise NotImplementedError
386:   end
387: 
388:   ##
389:   # @return [Class]
390:   #  Returns the class responsible for decoding streamed response bodies
391:   def stream_decoder
392:     LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder
393:   end
394: 
395:   ##
396:   # Resolves tools to their function representations
397:   # @param [Array<LLM::Function, LLM::Tool>] tools
398:   #  The tools to map
399:   # @raise [TypeError]
400:   #  When a tool is not recognized
401:   # @return [Array<LLM::Function>]
402:   def resolve_tools(tools)
403:     (tools || []).map do |tool|
404:       if tool.respond_to?(:function)
405:         tool.function
406:       elsif [LLM::Function, LLM::ServerTool, Hash].any? { _1 === tool }
407:         tool
408:       else
409:         raise TypeError, "#{tool.class} given as a tool but it is not recognized"
410:       end
411:     end
412:   end
413: 
414:   ##
415:   # @api private
416:   def lock(&)
417:     @monitor.synchronize(&)
418:   end
419: 
420:   ##
421:   # @api private
422:   def thread
423:     Thread.current
424:   end
425:

428:   def weakmap
429:     thread[:"llm.provider.weakmap"] ||= ObjectSpace::WeakMap.new

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::Transport LLM::Transport} class defines the execution
6:   # interface used by {LLM::Provider}.
7:   #
8:   # Custom transports can subclass this class and override {#request} to
9:   # execute provider requests without changing request adapters or
10:   # response adapters.
11:   #
12:   # Providers should construct {LLM::Transport::Request} objects before
13:   # delegating to a transport. Custom transports can execute those
14:   # requests directly, or transform them into backend-specific request
15:   # objects before execution.
16:   #
17:   # Only {#request} is required. The remaining methods are optional hooks
18:   # for features such as interruption, request ownership, or persistence,
19:   # and only need to be implemented when the underlying adapter can
20:   # support them.
21:   #
22:   # Returned responses should implement the
23:   # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} interface. In
24:   # practice this can mean adapting another client's response object so
25:   # existing provider execution, response adapters, and error handlers
26:   # can rely on one normalized response contract instead of
27:   # transport-specific classes.
28:   class Transport
29:     require_relative "transport/request"
30:     require_relative "transport/response"
31:     require_relative "transport/utils"
32:     require_relative "transport/stream_decoder"
33:     require_relative "transport/net_http_adapter"
34:     require_relative "transport/http"
35:     require_relative "transport/persistent_http"
36:     require_relative "transport/curb"
37:     require_relative "transport/execution"
38: 
39:     ##
40:     # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP transport class.
41:     # @return [Class]
42:     def self.net_http
43:       HTTP
44:     end
45: 
46:     ##
47:     # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP::Persistent transport class.
48:     # @return [Class]
49:     def self.net_http_persistent
50:       PersistentHTTP
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # Returns the optional libcurl (curb) transport class.
55:     # Requires the `curb` gem.
56:     # @return [Class]
57:     def self.curb
58:       Curb
59:     end
60: 
61:     ##
62:     # Performs a request through the transport.
63:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
64:     # @param [Object] owner
65:     # @param [LLM::Object, nil] stream
66:     # @yieldparam [LLM::Transport::Response] response
67:     # @return [Object]
68:     def request(request, owner:, stream: nil, &)
69:       raise NotImplementedError
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # Returns the current request owner.
74:     # @return [Object]
75:     def request_owner
76:       return Fiber.current unless defined?(::Async)
77:       Async::Task.current? ? Async::Task.current : Fiber.current
78:     end
79: 
80:     ##
81:     # Returns the exception classes that indicate an interrupted request.
82:     # @return [Array<Class<Exception>>]
83:     def interrupt_errors
84:       []
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # Interrupt an active request, if any.
89:     # @param [Object] owner
90:     # @return [nil]
91:     def interrupt!(owner)
92:       raise NotImplementedError
93:     end
94: 
95:     ##
96:     # Returns whether an execution owner was interrupted.
97:     # @param [Object] owner
98:     # @return [Boolean, nil]
99:     def interrupted?(owner)
100:       nil
101:     end
102: 
103:     ##
104:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
105:     # @param [IO] io
106:     # @return [void]
107:     def set_body_stream(request, io)
108:       request.body_stream = io
109:       request["transfer-encoding"] = "chunked" unless request["content-length"]
110:     end
111:   end
112: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Response LLM::Response} is the normalized base shape for
6:   # provider and endpoint responses in llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # Provider calls return an instance of this class, then extend it
9:   # with provider-, endpoint-, or context-specific modules so response
10:   # handling can share one common surface without flattening away
11:   # specialized behavior.
12:   #
13:   # The normalized response keeps the transport response available
14:   # through {#res}. When the default net/http transport is in use,
15:   # {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP
16:   # LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP} keeps the
17:   # original {Net::HTTPResponse Net::HTTPResponse} available through
18:   # its own {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP#res #res}.
19:   class Response
20:     require "json"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # Returns the HTTP response
24:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
25:     attr_reader :res
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
29:     #  HTTP response
30:     # @return [LLM::Response]
31:     #  Returns an instance of LLM::Response
32:     def initialize(res)
33:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
34:     end
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # Returns the response body
38:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
39:     #  Returns an LLM::Object when the response body is JSON,
40:     #  otherwise returns a raw string.
41:     def body
42:       @res.body
43:     end
44: 
45:     ##
46:     # Returns an inspection of the response object
47:     # @return [String]
48:     def inspect
49:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @body=#{body.inspect} @res=#{@res.inspect}>"
50:     end
51: 
52:     ##
53:     # Returns true if the response is successful
54:     # @return [Boolean]
55:     def ok?
56:       @res.success?
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Returns true if the response is from the Files API
61:     # @return [Boolean]
62:     def file?
63:       false
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     def method_missing(m, *args, **kwargs, &b)
69:       if LLM::Object === body
70:         body.respond_to?(m) ? body[m.to_s] : super
71:       else
72:         super
73:       end
74:     end
75: 
76:     def respond_to_missing?(m, include_private = false)
77:       if LLM::Object === body
78:         body.respond_to?(m)
79:       else
80:         false
81:       end
82:     end
83:   end
84: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The superclass of all LLM errors
6:   class Error < RuntimeError
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response, nil]
9:     #  Returns the response associated with an error, or nil
10:     attr_accessor :response
11: 
12:     def initialize(...)
13:       block_given? ? yield(self) : nil
14:       super
15:     end
16: 
17:     def message
18:       if response
19:         [super, response.body].join("\n")
20:       else
21:         super
22:       end
23:     end
24:   end
25: 
26:   ##
27:   # HTTPUnauthorized
28:   UnauthorizedError = Class.new(Error)
29: 
30:   ##
31:   # HTTPTooManyRequests
32:   RateLimitError = Class.new(Error)
33: 
34:   ##
35:   # HTTPServerError
36:   ServerError = Class.new(Error)
37: 
38:   ##
39:   # HTTPNotFound
40:   NotFoundError = Class.new(Error)
41: 
42:   ##
43:   # When an given an input object that is not understood
44:   FormatError = Class.new(Error)
45: 
46:   ##
47:   # When given a prompt object that is not understood
48:   PromptError = Class.new(FormatError)
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # When given an invalid request
52:   InvalidRequestError = Class.new(Error)
53: 
54:   ##
55:   # When the context window is exceeded
56:   ContextWindowError = Class.new(InvalidRequestError)
57: 
58:   ##
59:   # When stuck in a tool call loop
60:   ToolLoopError = Class.new(Error)
61: 
62:   ##
63:   # When a guard blocks pending tool execution
64:   GuardError = Class.new(Error)
65: 
66:   ##
67:   # When a request is interrupted
68:   Interrupt = Class.new(Error)
69: 
70:   ##
71:   # When a concurrency strategy cannot execute a given tool
72:   RactorError = Class.new(Error)
73: 
74:   ##
75:   # When a tool call cannot be mapped to a local tool
76:   NoSuchToolError = Class.new(Error)
77: 
78:   ##
79:   # When {LLM::Registry} can't map a model
80:   NoSuchModelError = Class.new(Error)
81: 
82:   ##
83:   # When {LLM::Registry} can't map a registry
84:   NoSuchRegistryError = Class.new(Error)
85: 
86:   ##
87:   # When an optional runtime dependency cannot be required
88:   LoadError = Class.new(Error)
89: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # The {LLM::Registry LLM::Registry} class provides a small API over
5: # provider model data. It exposes model metadata such as pricing,
6: # capabilities, modalities, and limits from the registry files
7: # stored under `data/`. The data is provided by https://models.dev
8: # and shipped with llm.rb.
9: class LLM::Registry
10:   @root = File.join(__dir__, "..", "..")
11: 
12:   ##
13:   # @raise [LLM::Error]
14:   #  Might raise an error
15:   # @param [Symbol]
16:   #  A provider name
17:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
18:   def self.for(name)
19:     path = File.join @root, "data", "#{name}.json"
20:     if File.file?(path)
21:       new LLM.json.load(File.binread(path))
22:     else
23:       raise LLM::NoSuchRegistryError, "no registry found for #{name}"
24:     end
25:   end
26: 
27:   ##
28:   # @param [Hash] blob
29:   #  A model registry
30:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
31:   def initialize(blob)
32:     @registry = LLM::Object.from(blob)
33:     @models = @registry.models
34:   end
35: 
36:   ##
37:   # @return [LLM::Object]
38:   #  Returns model costs
39:   def cost(model:)
40:     lookup(model:).cost
41:   end
42: 
43:   ##
44:   # @return [LLM::Object]
45:   #  Returns model modalities
46:   def modalities(model:)
47:     lookup(model:).modalities
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @return [LLM::Object]
52:   #  Returns model limits such as the context window size
53:   def limit(model:)
54:     lookup(model:).limit
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def lookup(model:)
60:     if @models.key?(model)
61:       @models[model]
62:     else
63:       patterns = {/-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ => "", /\A(gpt-.*)-\d{4}$/ => "\\1"}
64:       fallback = find_map(patterns) { model.dup.sub!(_1, _2) } || "none"
65:       if @models.key?(fallback)
66:         @models[fallback]
67:       else
68:         raise LLM::NoSuchModelError, "no such model: #{model} (fallback: #{fallback})"
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   ##
74:   # Similar to #{find} but returns the block's return value
75:   # @return [Object, nil]
76:   def find_map(pair)
77:     result = nil
78:     pair.each_pair { break if result = yield(_1, _2) }
79:     result
80:   end
81: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # The {LLM::Model LLM::Model} class provides a normalized view of
5: # a provider model record returned by the models API.
6: class LLM::Model
7:   ##
8:   # The provider-specific model payload.
9:   # @return [LLM::Object]
10:   attr_reader :raw
11: 
12:   ##
13:   # @param [LLM::Object, Hash] raw
14:   def initialize(raw)
15:     @raw = raw
16:   end
17: 
18:   ##
19:   # Returns a normalized identifier suitable for API calls.
20:   # @return [String, nil]
21:   def id
22:     normalize_id(raw.id || raw.model || raw.name)
23:   end
24: 
25:   ##
26:   # Returns a display-friendly model name.
27:   # @return [String, nil]
28:   def name
29:     raw.display_name || raw.displayName || id
30:   end
31: 
32:   ##
33:   # Best-effort predicate for chat support.
34:   # @return [Boolean]
35:   def chat?
36:     return true if anthropic?
37:     return [*(raw.supportedGenerationMethods || [])].include?("generateContent") if google?
38:     openai_compatible_chat?
39:   end
40: 
41:   ##
42:   # Returns a Hash representation of the normalized model.
43:   # @return [Hash]
44:   def to_h
45:     {id:, name:, chat?: chat?}.compact
46:   end
47: 
48:   ##
49:   # @private
50:   module Collection
51:     include ::Enumerable
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # @yield [model]
55:     # @yieldparam [LLM::Model] model
56:     # @return [Enumerator, void]
57:     def each(&)
58:       return enum_for(:each) unless block_given?
59:       models.each(&)
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Returns an element, or a slice, or nil.
64:     # @return [Object, Array<Object>, nil]
65:     def [](*pos, **kw)
66:       models[*pos, **kw]
67:     end
68: 
69:     ##
70:     # @return [Boolean]
71:     def empty?
72:       models.empty?
73:     end
74: 
75:     ##
76:     # @return [Integer]
77:     def size
78:       models.size
79:     end
80: 
81:     ##
82:     # Returns normalized models.
83:     # @return [Array<LLM::Model>]
84:     def models
85:       @models ||= raw_models.map { LLM::Model.new(_1) }
86:     end
87:   end
88: 
89:   private
90: 
91:   def normalize_id(value)
92:     value&.sub(%r{\Amodels/}, "")
93:   end
94: 
95:   def anthropic?
96:     raw.type == "model" && raw.key?(:display_name) && raw.key?(:created_at)
97:   end
98: 
99:   def google?
100:     raw.key?(:supportedGenerationMethods)
101:   end
102: 
103:   def openai_compatible_chat?
104:     value = [id, raw.name, raw.model].compact.join(" ").downcase
105:     return false if value.include?("embedding")
106:     return false if value.include?("moderation")
107:     return false if value.include?("tts")
108:     return false if value.include?("transcrib")
109:     return false if value.include?("image")
110:     return false if value.include?("whisper")
111:     return false if value.include?("dall")
112:     return false if value.include?("omni-moderation")
113:     true
114:   end
115: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # Internal request execution methods for {LLM::Provider}.
6:   #
7:   # This module handles provider-side transport execution, response
8:   # parsing, streaming, and request body setup.
9:   #
10:   # @api private
11:   module Execution
12:     private
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Executes a HTTP request
16:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
17:     #  The request to send
18:     # @param [Proc] b
19:     #  A block to yield the response to (optional)
20:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
21:     #  The response from the server
22:     # @raise [LLM::Error::Unauthorized]
23:     #  When authentication fails
24:     # @raise [LLM::Error::RateLimit]
25:     #  When the rate limit is exceeded
26:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
27:     #  When any other unsuccessful status code is returned
28:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
29:     #  When there is a network error at the operating system level
30:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
31:     def execute(request:, operation:, stream: nil, stream_parser: self.stream_parser, model: nil, inputs: nil, &b)
32:       stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
33:       owner = transport.request_owner
34:       tracer = self.tracer
35:       span = tracer.on_request_start(operation:, model:, inputs:)
36:       res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
37:       res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
38:       [handle_response(res, tracer, span), span, tracer]
39:     rescue *transport.interrupt_errors
40:       raise LLM::Interrupt, "request interrupted" if transport.interrupted?(owner)
41:       raise
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # Handles the response from a request
46:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
47:     #  The response to handle
48:     # @param [Object, nil] span
49:     #  The span
50:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
51:     def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
52:       res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
53:       res
54:     end
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Parse a HTTP response
58:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
59:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
60:     def parse_response(res)
61:       case res["content-type"]
62:       when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
63:       else res.body
64:       end
65:     end
66:   end
67: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Transport::Request LLM::Transport::Request} defines the
6:   # normalized request interface expected by transports.
7:   #
8:   # Providers build request objects through this class, then hand them
9:   # to a transport for execution without depending on any specific HTTP
10:   # client library.
11:   class Request
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     attr_accessor :body
15: 
16:     ##
17:     # @return [IO, nil]
18:     attr_accessor :body_stream
19: 
20:     ##
21:     # @return [String]
22:     attr_reader :method
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @return [String]
26:     attr_reader :path
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @return [Hash]
30:     attr_reader :headers
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # @param [String] path
34:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
35:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
36:     def self.get(path, headers = nil)
37:       new("GET", path, headers)
38:     end
39: 
40:     ##
41:     # @param [String] path
42:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
43:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
44:     def self.post(path, headers = nil)
45:       new("POST", path, headers)
46:     end
47: 
48:     ##
49:     # @param [String] path
50:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
51:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
52:     def self.put(path, headers = nil)
53:       new("PUT", path, headers)
54:     end
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # @param [String] path
58:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
59:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
60:     def self.patch(path, headers = nil)
61:       new("PATCH", path, headers)
62:     end
63: 
64:     ##
65:     # @param [String] path
66:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
67:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
68:     def self.delete(path, headers = nil)
69:       new("DELETE", path, headers)
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @param [String] method
74:     # @param [String] path
75:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
76:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
77:     def initialize(method, path, headers = nil)
78:       @method = method.to_s.upcase
79:       @path = path.to_s
80:       @headers = {}
81:       (headers || {}).each { self[_1] = _2 }
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # @param [String] key
86:     # @return [String, nil]
87:     def [](key)
88:       @headers[normalize_header(key)]
89:     end
90: 
91:     ##
92:     # @param [String] key
93:     # @param [Object] value
94:     # @return [String]
95:     def []=(key, value)
96:       @headers[normalize_header(key)] = value.to_s
97:     end
98: 
99:     ##
100:     # @yieldparam [String] key
101:     # @yieldparam [String] value
102:     # @return [Hash]
103:     def each_header(&block)
104:       @headers.each(&block)
105:     end
106: 
107:     ##
108:     # @return [String]
109:     def inspect
110:       "#<#{self.class.name}:0x#{object_id.to_s(16)}" \
111:         " @method=#{@method} @path=#{@path}" \
112:         " @headers=#{@headers.inspect}>"
113:     end
114: 
115:     private
116: 
117:     def normalize_header(key)
118:       key.to_s.downcase
119:     end
120:   end
121: end

3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} defines the
6:   # normalized HTTP response interface expected by transports and
7:   # provider error handlers.
8:   #

10:   # client, then adapt that client's native response object to this
11:   # interface.
12:   #
13:   # This keeps the transport boundary focused on one contract:
14:   # providers, execution, and error handlers only need a response
15:   # object that implements
16:   # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response}, regardless of
17:   # how the request was actually performed.

25:     def self.from(res)
26:       return res if LLM::Transport::Response === res
27:       return HTTP.new(res) if Net::HTTPResponse === res
28:       res
29:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "net/http"
4: 
5: class LLM::Transport
6:   ##
7:   # The {LLM::Transport::HTTP LLM::Transport::HTTP} transport is the
8:   # built-in adapter for Ruby's {Net::HTTP Net::HTTP}. It manages
9:   # transient HTTP connections, tracks active requests by owner, and
10:   # interrupts in-flight requests when needed.
11:   #
12:   # @api private
13:   class HTTP < self
14:     include NetHTTPAdapter
15: 
16:     INTERRUPT_ERRORS = [::IOError, ::EOFError, Errno::EBADF].freeze
17:     ActiveRequest = Struct.new(:client, keyword_init: true)
18: 
19:     ##
20:     # @param [String] host
21:     # @param [Integer] port
22:     # @param [Integer] timeout
23:     # @param [Boolean] ssl
24:     # @return [LLM::Transport::HTTP]
25:     def initialize(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:)
26:       @host = host
27:       @port = port
28:       @timeout = timeout
29:       @ssl = ssl
30:       @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
31:       @monitor = Monitor.new
32:     end
33: 
34:     ##
35:     # Returns the current request owner.
36:     # @return [Object]
37:     def request_owner
38:       return Fiber.current unless defined?(::Async)
39:       Async::Task.current? ? Async::Task.current : Fiber.current
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # @return [Array<Class<Exception>>]
44:     def interrupt_errors
45:       [*INTERRUPT_ERRORS, *optional_interrupt_errors]
46:     end
47: 
48:     ##
49:     # Interrupt an active request, if any.
50:     # @param [Fiber] owner
51:     # @return [nil]
52:     def interrupt!(owner)
53:       req = request_for(owner) or return
54:       lock { (@interrupts ||= {})[owner] = true }
55:       close_socket(req.client)
56:       req.client.finish if req.client.active?
57:       owner.stop if owner.respond_to?(:stop)
58:     rescue *interrupt_errors
59:       nil
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Returns whether an execution owner was interrupted.
64:     # @param [Fiber] owner
65:     # @return [Boolean, nil]
66:     def interrupted?(owner)
67:       lock { @interrupts&.delete(owner) }
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Performs a request on the current HTTP transport.
72:     # Accepts both {Net::HTTPRequest} and {LLM::Transport::Request}.
73:     #
74:     # @param [Net::HTTPRequest, LLM::Transport::Request] request
75:     # @param [Fiber] owner
76:     # @param [LLM::Object, nil] stream
77:     # @yieldparam [LLM::Transport::Response] response
78:     # @return [Object]
79:     def request(request, owner:, stream: nil, &b)
80:       http_req = resolve_request(request)
81:       client = client()
82:       set_request(ActiveRequest.new(client:), owner)
83:       perform_request(client, http_req, stream, &b)
84:     ensure
85:       clear_request(owner)
86:     end
87: 
88:     ##
89:     # @return [String]
90:     def inspect
91:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)}>"
92:     end
93: 
94:     private
95: 
96:     attr_reader :host, :port, :timeout, :ssl, :base_uri
97: 
98:     def client
99:       client = Net::HTTP.new(host, port)
100:       client.read_timeout = timeout
101:       client.use_ssl = ssl
102:       client
103:     end
104: 
105:     def close_socket(http)
106:       socket = http&.instance_variable_get(:@socket) or return
107:       socket = socket.io if socket.respond_to?(:io)
108:       socket.close
109:     rescue *interrupt_errors
110:       nil
111:     end
112: 
113:     def request_for(owner)
114:       lock do
115:         @requests ||= {}
116:         @requests[owner]
117:       end
118:     end
119: 
120:     def set_request(req, owner)
121:       lock do
122:         @requests ||= {}
123:         @requests[owner] = req
124:       end
125:     end
126: 
127:     def clear_request(owner)
128:       lock { @requests&.delete(owner) }
129:     end
130: 
131:     def lock(&)
132:       @monitor.synchronize(&)
133:     end
134: 
135:     def optional_interrupt_errors
136:       defined?(::Async::Stop) ? [Async::Stop] : []
137:     end
138:   end
139: end

(End of file - total 139 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Context LLM::Context} is the stateful execution boundary in
6:   # llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # It holds the evolving runtime state for an LLM workflow:
9:   # conversation history, tool calls and returns, schema and streaming
10:   # configuration, accumulated usage, and request ownership for
11:   # interruption.
12:   #
13:   # This is broader than prompt context alone. A context is the object
14:   # that lets one-off prompts, streaming turns, tool execution,
15:   # persistence, retries, and serialized long-lived workflows all run
16:   # through the same model.
17:   #
18:   # A context can drive the chat completions API that all providers
19:   # support or the Responses API on providers that expose it.
20:   #
21:   # @example
22:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
26:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
27:   #
28:   #   prompt = LLM::Prompt.new(llm) do
29:   #     system "Be concise and show your reasoning briefly."
30:   #     user "If a train goes 60 mph for 1.5 hours, how far does it travel?"
31:   #     user "Now double the speed for the same time."
32:   #   end
33:   #
34:   #   ctx.talk(prompt)
35:   #   ctx.messages.each { |m| puts "[#{m.role}] #{m.content}" }
36:   class Context
37:     require_relative "compactor"
38:     require_relative "context/serializer"
39:     require_relative "context/deserializer"
40:     include Serializer
41:     include Deserializer
42: 
43:     ZERO_USAGE = LLM::Object.from(
44:       input_tokens: 0,
45:       output_tokens: 0,
46:       reasoning_tokens: 0,
47:       input_audio_tokens: 0,
48:       output_audio_tokens: 0,
49:       input_image_tokens: 0,
50:       cache_read_tokens: 0,
51:       cache_write_tokens: 0,
52:       total_tokens: 0
53:     )
54:     private_constant :ZERO_USAGE
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Returns the accumulated message history for this context
58:     # @return [LLM::Buffer<LLM::Message>]
59:     attr_reader :messages
60: 
61:     ##
62:     # Returns a provider
63:     # @return [LLM::Provider]
64:     attr_reader :llm
65: 
66:     ##
67:     # Returns the context mode
68:     # @return [Symbol]
69:     attr_reader :mode
70: 
71:     ##
72:     # @param [LLM::Provider] llm
73:     #  A provider
74:     # @param [Hash] params
75:     #  The parameters to maintain throughout the conversation.
76:     #  Any parameter the provider supports can be included and
77:     #  not only those listed here.
78:     # @option params [Symbol] :mode Defaults to :completions
79:     # @option params [String] :model Defaults to the provider's default model
80:     # @option params [Array<LLM::Function>, nil] :tools Defaults to nil
81:     # @option params [Array<String>, nil] :skills Defaults to nil
82:     def initialize(llm, params = {})
83:       @llm = llm
84:       @mode = params.delete(:mode) || :completions
85:       @compactor = params.delete(:compactor)
86:       @guard = params.delete(:guard)
87:       @transformer = params.delete(:transformer)
88:       tools = [*params.delete(:tools), *load_skills(params.delete(:skills))]
89:       @params = {model: llm.default_model, schema: nil}.compact.merge!(params)
90:       @params[:tools] = tools unless tools.empty?
91:       @messages = LLM::Buffer.new(llm)
92:     end
93: 
94:     ##
95:     # Returns the default params for this context
96:     # @return [Hash]
97:     def params
98:       @params.dup
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # Returns a context compactor
103:     # This feature is inspired by the compaction approach developed by
104:     # General Intelligence Systems.
105:     # @return [LLM::Compactor]
106:     def compactor
107:       @compactor = LLM::Compactor.new(self, @compactor || {}) unless LLM::Compactor === @compactor
108:       @compactor
109:     end
110: 
111:     ##
112:     # Sets a context compactor or compactor config
113:     # @param [LLM::Compactor, Hash, nil] compactor
114:     # @return [LLM::Compactor, Hash, nil]
115:     def compactor=(compactor)
116:       @compactor = compactor
117:     end
118: 
119:     ##
120:     # Returns whether the context has been compacted and no later model
121:     # response has cleared that state.
122:     # @return [Boolean]
123:     # @api private
124:     attr_accessor :compacted
125:     alias_method :compacted?, :compacted
126: 
127:     ##
128:     # Returns a guard, if configured.
129:     #
130:     # Guards are context-level supervisors for agentic execution. A guard can
131:     # inspect the runtime state and decide whether pending tool work should be
132:     # blocked before the context keeps looping.
133:     #
134:     # The built-in implementation is {LLM::LoopGuard LLM::LoopGuard}, which
135:     # detects repeated tool-call patterns and turns them into in-band
136:     # {LLM::GuardError LLM::GuardError} tool returns.
137:     #
138:     # @return [#call, nil]
139:     def guard
140:       return if @guard.nil? || @guard == false
141:       @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new if @guard == true
142:       @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new(@guard) if Hash === @guard
143:       @guard
144:     end
145: 
146:     ##
147:     # Sets a guard or guard config.
148:     #
149:     # Guards must implement `call(ctx)` and return either `nil` or a warning
150:     # string. Returning a warning tells the context to block pending tool work
151:     # with guarded tool errors instead of continuing the loop.
152:     #
153:     # @param [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil] guard
154:     # @return [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil]
155:     def guard=(guard)
156:       @guard = guard
157:     end
158: 
159:     ##
160:     # Returns a transformer, if configured.
161:     #
162:     # Transformers can rewrite outgoing prompts and params before a request is
163:     # sent to the provider.
164:     #
165:     # @return [#call, nil]
166:     def transformer
167:       @transformer
168:     end
169: 
170:     ##
171:     # Sets a transformer.
172:     #
173:     # Transformers must implement `call(ctx, prompt, params)` and return a
174:     # two-element array of `[prompt, params]`.
175:     #
176:     # @param [#call, nil] transformer
177:     # @return [#call, nil]
178:     def transformer=(transformer)
179:       @transformer = transformer
180:     end
181: 
182:     # Interact with the context via the chat completions API.
183:     # This method immediately sends a request to the LLM and returns the response.
184:     #
185:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
186:     # @param params The params, including optional :role (defaults to :user), :stream, :tools, :schema etc.
187:     # @return [LLM::Response] Returns the LLM's response for this turn.
188:     # @example
189:     #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
190:     #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
191:     #   res = ctx.talk("Hello, what is your name?")
192:     #   puts res.messages[0].content
193:     def talk(prompt, params = {})
194:       @owner = @llm.request_owner
195:       compactor.compact!(prompt) if compactor.compact?(prompt)
196:       repair!(@messages, prompt)
197:       prompt, params, res = mode == :responses ? respond(prompt, params) : complete(prompt, params)
198:       self.compacted = false
199:       role = params[:role] || @llm.user_role
200:       role = @llm.tool_role if params[:role].nil? && [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
201:       @messages.concat LLM::Prompt === prompt ? prompt.to_a : [LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
202:       @messages.concat [res.choices[-1]]
203:       res
204:     end
205: 
206:     ##
207:     # Ask a question and return the content string directly.
208:     # Accepts `with:` for file attachments and a block for streaming.
209:     # This interface is compatible with RubyLLM's `ask` method.
210:     # @param [String] prompt
211:     # @param [Hash] options
212:     # @option options [String, Array<String>, nil] :with
213:     #  File path(s) to attach
214:     # @option options [#<<, LLM::Stream, nil] :stream
215:     #  A stream target
216:     # @yield [String] content chunks when streaming
217:     # @return [LLM::Response]
218:     def ask(prompt, options = {}, &block)
219:       options = {with: nil, stream: nil}.merge!(options || {})
220:       with, stream = options.values_at(:with, :stream)
221:       prompt = with ? [prompt, [*with].map { local_file(_1) }] : prompt
222:       target = if block
223:         blk = block.dup
224:         blk.singleton_class.alias_method(:<<, :call)
225:         blk
226:       else
227:         stream
228:       end
229:       target ? talk(prompt, stream: target) : talk(prompt)
230:     end
231: 
232:     ##
233:     # @return [String]
234:     def inspect
235:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} " \
236:       "@llm=#{@llm.class}, @mode=#{@mode.inspect}, @params=#{@params.inspect}, " \
237:       "@messages=#{@messages.inspect}>"
238:     end
239: 
240:     ##
241:     # Returns an array of functions that can be called
242:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function>]
243:     def functions
244:       return_ids = returns.map(&:id)
245:       @messages
246:         .select(&:assistant?)
247:         .flat_map do |msg|
248:           fns = msg.functions.select { _1.pending? && !return_ids.include?(_1.id) }
249:           fns.each do |fn|
250:             fn.tracer = tracer
251:             fn.model  = msg.model
252:           end
253:         end.extend(LLM::Function::Array)
254:     end
255: 
256:     ##
257:     # Returns whether there is pending tool work in this context.
258:     # This prefers queued streamed tool work when present, and otherwise
259:     # falls back to unresolved functions derived from the message history.
260:     # @return [Boolean]
261:     def functions?
262:       pending = queue
263:       (pending && !pending.empty?) || functions.any?
264:     end
265: 
266:     ##
267:     # Spawns a function through the context.
268:     #
269:     # When a guard is configured, this method can return an in-band guarded
270:     # tool error instead of spawning work.
271:     #
272:     # @param [LLM::Function] function
273:     # @param [Symbol] strategy
274:     # @return [LLM::Function::Return, LLM::Function::Task]
275:     def spawn(function, strategy)
276:       warning = guard&.call(self)
277:       return guarded_return_for(function, warning) if warning
278:       function.spawn(strategy)
279:     end
280: 
281:     ##
282:     # Returns tool returns accumulated in this context
283:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
284:     def returns
285:       @messages
286:         .select(&:tool_return?)
287:         .flat_map do |msg|
288:           LLM::Function::Return === msg.content ?
289:             [msg.content] :
290:             [*msg.content].grep(LLM::Function::Return)
291:         end
292:     end
293: 
294:     ##
295:     # Waits for queued tool work to finish.
296:     #
297:     # This prefers queued streamed tool work when the configured stream
298:     # exposes a non-empty queue. Otherwise it falls back to waiting on
299:     # the context's pending functions directly.
300:     #
301:     # @param [Symbol, Array<Symbol>] strategy
302:     #  If the stream queue already has tool work, `wait` will drain it
303:     #  without using this argument.
304:     #  Otherwise, this controls how pending functions are resolved directly.
305:     #  Use `:call` for sequential execution without spawning.
306:     # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] except
307:     #  A list of functions to exclude from the wait
308:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
309:     def wait(strategy, except: [])
310:       if LLM::Stream === stream && !stream.queue.empty?
311:         @queue = stream.queue
312:         @queue.wait
313:       else
314:         tools  = except.empty? ? functions : functions - except
315:         guards = guarded_returns(tools:)
316:         return guards if guards
317:         @queue = tools.spawn(strategy)
318:         returns = @queue.wait
319:         emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)
320:         returns
321:       end
322:     ensure
323:       @queue = nil
324:       @stream = nil
325:     end
326: 
327:     ##
328:     # Interrupt the active request, if any.
329:     # This is inspired by Go's context cancellation model.
330:     # @return [nil]
331:     def interrupt!
332:       pending = functions.to_a
333:       llm.interrupt!(@owner)
334:       queue&.interrupt!
335:       return if pending.empty?
336:       pending.each(&:interrupt!)
337:       returns = pending.map { _1.cancel(reason: "function call cancelled") }
338:       @messages << LLM::Message.new(@llm.tool_role, returns)
339:       nil
340:     end
341:     alias_method :cancel!, :interrupt!
342: 
343:     ##
344:     # Returns token usage accumulated in this context
345:     # @return [LLM::Object]
346:     def usage
347:       if usage = @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.usage
348:         LLM::Object.from(
349:           input_tokens: usage.input_tokens || 0,
350:           output_tokens: usage.output_tokens || 0,
351:           reasoning_tokens: usage.reasoning_tokens || 0,
352:           input_audio_tokens: usage.input_audio_tokens || 0,
353:           output_audio_tokens: usage.output_audio_tokens || 0,
354:           input_image_tokens: usage.input_image_tokens || 0,
355:           cache_read_tokens: usage.cache_read_tokens || 0,
356:           cache_write_tokens: usage.cache_write_tokens || 0,
357:           total_tokens: usage.total_tokens || 0
358:         )
359:       else
360:         ZERO_USAGE
361:       end
362:     end
363: 
364:     ##
365:     # Build a role-aware prompt for a single request.
366:     #
367:     # Prefer this method over {#build_prompt}. The older
368:     # method name is kept for backward compatibility.
369:     # @example
370:     #   prompt = ctx.prompt do
371:     #     system "Your task is to assist the user"
372:     #     user "Hello, can you assist me?"
373:     #   end
374:     #   ctx.talk(prompt)
375:     # @param [Proc] b
376:     #  A block that composes messages. If it takes one argument,
377:     #  it receives the prompt object. Otherwise it runs in prompt context.
378:     # @return [LLM::Prompt]
379:     def prompt(&b)
380:       LLM::Prompt.new(@llm, &b)
381:     end
382:     alias_method :build_prompt, :prompt
383: 
384:     ##
385:     # Recongize an object as a URL to an image
386:     # @param [String] url
387:     #  The URL
388:     # @return [LLM::Object]
389:     #  Returns a tagged object
390:     def image_url(url)
391:       LLM::Object.from(value: url, kind: :image_url)
392:     end
393: 
394:     ##
395:     # Recongize an object as a local file
396:     # @param [String] path
397:     #  The path
398:     # @return [LLM::Object]
399:     #  Returns a tagged object
400:     def local_file(path)
401:       LLM::Object.from(value: LLM.File(path), kind: :local_file)
402:     end
403: 
404:     ##
405:     # Reconginize an object as a remote file
406:     # @param [LLM::Response] res
407:     #  The response
408:     # @return [LLM::Object]
409:     #  Returns a tagged object
410:     def remote_file(res)
411:       LLM::Object.from(value: res, kind: :remote_file)
412:     end
413: 
414:     ##
415:     # @return [LLM::Tracer]
416:     #  Returns an LLM tracer
417:     def tracer
418:       @llm.tracer
419:     end
420: 
421:     ##
422:     # @return [LLM::Stream, #<<, nil]
423:     #  Returns a stream object, or nil
424:     def stream
425:       @stream || @params[:stream]
426:     end
427: 
428:     ##
429:     # Returns the model a Context is actively using
430:     # @return [String]
431:     def model
432:       messages.find(&:assistant?)&.model || @params[:model]
433:     end
434: 
435:     ##
436:     # @return [Hash]
437:     def to_h
438:       {
439:         schema_version: 1,
440:         model:,
441:         compacted:,
442:         messages: @messages.map { serialize_message(_1) }
443:       }
444:     end
445: 
446:     ##
447:     # @return [String]
448:     def to_json(...)
449:       LLM.json.dump(to_h, ...)
450:     end
451: 
452:     ##
453:     # Save the current context state
454:     # @example
455:     #  llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
456:     #  ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
457:     #  ctx.talk "Hello"
458:     #  ctx.save(path: "context.json")
459:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
460:     #  Might raise a number of SystemCallError subclasses
461:     # @return [void]
462:     def serialize(path:)
463:       ::File.binwrite path, LLM.json.dump(to_h)
464:     end
465:     alias_method :save, :serialize
466: 
467:     ##
468:     # @return [LLM::Cost]
469:     #  Returns an _approximate_ cost for a given context
470:     #  based on both the provider, and model
471:     def cost
472:       LLM::Cost.from(self)
473:     end
474: 
475:     ##
476:     # Returns the model's context window.
477:     # The context window is the maximum amount of input and output
478:     # tokens a model can consider in a single request.
479:     # @note
480:     #   This method returns 0 when the provider or
481:     #   model can't be found within {LLM::Registry}.
482:     # @return [Integer]
483:     def context_window
484:       LLM
485:         .registry_for(llm)
486:         .limit(model:)
487:         .context
488:     rescue LLM::NoSuchModelError, LLM::NoSuchRegistryError
489:       0
490:     end
491: 
492:     private
493: 
494:     ##
495:     # Binds runtime metadata onto an active stream.
496:     # @api private
497:     def bind!(stream, model, tools)
498:       return unless LLM::Stream === stream
499:       @stream = stream
500:       stream.extra[:ctx] = self
501:       stream.extra[:tracer] = tracer
502:       stream.extra[:model] = model
503:       stream.extra[:tools] = tools
504:     end
505: 
506:     ##
507:     # Returns the bound stream queue, if available.
508:     # @api private
509:     def queue
510:       [@queue, stream&.queue].compact.first
511:     rescue NoMethodError
512:       nil
513:     end
514: 
515:     ##
516:     # Loads skill directories and adapts them into tools.
517:     # @api private
518:     def load_skills(skills)
519:       [*skills].map { LLM::Skill.load(_1).to_tool(self) }
520:     end
521: 
522:     ##
523:     # Builds in-band guarded returns when the guard blocks tool work.
524:     # @api private
525:     def guarded_returns(tools:)
526:       warning = guard&.call(self)
527:       return unless warning
528:       tools.map { guarded_return_for(_1, warning) }
529:     end
530: 
531:     ##
532:     # Rewrites a prompt and params through the configured transformer.
533:     # @api private
534:     def transform(prompt, params)
535:       return [prompt, params] unless transformer
536:       stream = params[:stream]
537:       stream.on_transform(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
538:       transformer.call(self, prompt, params)
539:     ensure
540:       stream.on_transform_finish(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
541:     end
542: 
543:     ##
544:     # Executes a turn through the Responses API.
545:     # @api private
546:     def respond(prompt, params)
547:       params = @params.merge(params)
548:       prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
549:       bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
550:       res_id = params[:store] == false ? nil : @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.response&.response_id
551:       params = params.merge(previous_response_id: res_id, input: @messages.to_a).compact
552:       [prompt, params, @llm.responses.create(prompt, params)]
553:     end
554: 
555:     ##
556:     # Executes a turn through the chat completions API.
557:     # @api private
558:     def complete(prompt, params)
559:       params = params.merge(messages: @messages.to_a)
560:       params = @params.merge(params)
561:       prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
562:       bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
563:       [prompt, params, @llm.complete(prompt, params)]
564:     end
565: 
566:     ##
567:     # Builds one guarded tool return for a blocked function call.
568:     # @api private
569:     def guarded_return_for(function, warning)
570:       LLM::Function::Return.new(function.id, function.name, {
571:         error: true,
572:         type: LLM::GuardError.name,
573:         message: warning
574:       })
575:     end
576: 
577:     ##
578:     # Emits tool return callbacks for directly waited function work.
579:     # @api private
580:     def emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)
581:       return unless LLM::Stream === stream
582:       returns.each_with_index { |result, index| stream.on_tool_return(tools[index], result) }
583:     end
584: 
585:     ##
586:     # Closes assistant tool-call messages that do not have matching tool
587:     # responses. This can happen when a turn is interrupted while a tool
588:     # call is streaming or waiting for user confirmation.
589:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
590:     # @param [Object] prompt
591:     # @return [void]
592:     def repair!(messages, prompt)
593:       message = messages.last
594:       return unless message&.tool_call?
595:       returns = self.returns + [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return)
596:       cancelled = []
597:       [*message.extra.tool_calls].each do |tool|
598:         next if returns.any? { _1.id == tool[:id] }
599:         attrs = {cancelled: true, reason: "function call cancelled"}
600:         cancelled << LLM::Function::Return.new(tool.id, tool.name, attrs)
601:       end
602:       messages << LLM::Message.new(@llm.tool_role, cancelled) unless cancelled.empty?
603:     end
604:   end
605: end

16:   class OpenAI < Provider

46:     ##
47:     # Provides an embedding
48:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create OpenAI docs
49:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
50:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
51:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
52:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
53:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#embed)
54:     def embed(input, model: "text-embedding-3-small", **params)
55:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path("/embeddings"), headers)
56:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:, model:}.merge!(params))
57:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
58:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
59:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
60:       res
61:     end

63:     ##
64:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
65:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create OpenAI docs
66:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
67:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
68:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
69:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
70:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
71:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
72:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
73:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
74:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
75:       req, messages = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
76:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
77:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
78:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
79:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
80:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
81:       res
82:     end

172:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
173:     # OpenAI web search tool.
174:     # @example
175:     #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
176:     #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
177:     #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
178:     # @param query [String] The search query.

182:         responses.create(query, store: false, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]),

210:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
211:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
212:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
213:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
214:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
215:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
216:       if params[:stream]
217:         params[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}.merge!(params[:stream_options] || {})
218:       end
219:       [params, stream, tools, role]
220:     end
221: 
222:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
223:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
224:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: adapt(messages, mode: :complete).flatten}.merge!(params))
225:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(completions_path, headers)
226:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
227:       [req, messages]
228:     end
229: 
230:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
231:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
232:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
233:       else
234:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
235:       end
236:     end
237: 
238:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
239:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
240:       value = message&.content || fallback
241:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
242:     end
243:   end
244: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Anthropic class implements a provider for
6:   # [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com).
7:   #
8:   # @example
9:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
10:   #   require "llm"
11:   #
12:   #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
13:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
14:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
15:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
16:   class Anthropic < Provider
17:     require_relative "anthropic/utils"
18:     require_relative "anthropic/error_handler"
19:     require_relative "anthropic/request_adapter"
20:     require_relative "anthropic/response_adapter"
21:     require_relative "anthropic/stream_parser"
22:     require_relative "anthropic/models"
23:     require_relative "anthropic/files"
24:     include RequestAdapter
25:     extend Utils
26: 
27:     HOST = "api.anthropic.com"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :anthropic
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
44:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/messages Anthropic docs
45:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
47:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
48:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
49:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
50:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
51:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
52:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
53:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
54:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
55:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
56:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
57:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
58:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
59:       res
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's models API
64:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/models-list
65:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Models]
66:     def models
67:       LLM::Anthropic::Models.new(self)
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's files API
72:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/files Anthropic docs
73:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Files]
74:     def files
75:       LLM::Anthropic::Files.new(self)
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
80:     def assistant_role
81:       "assistant"
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Anthropic expects tool results to be sent as user messages
86:     # containing `tool_result` content blocks rather than a distinct
87:     # `tool` role.
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
89:     def tool_role
90:       :user
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/all-models#model-comparison-table claude-sonnet-4-20250514
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # @note
103:     #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
104:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
105:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
106:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/tool-use/web-search-tool Anthropic docs
107:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
108:     def server_tools
109:       {
110:         bash: server_tool(:bash, type: "bash_20250124"),
111:         web_search: server_tool(:web_search, type: "web_search_20250305", max_uses: 5),
112:         text_editor: server_tool(:str_replace_based_edit_tool, type: "text_editor_20250728", max_characters: 10_000)
113:       }
114:     end
115: 
116:     ##
117:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
118:     # Anthropic web search tool.
119:     # @example
120:     #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
121:     #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
122:     #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
123:     # @param query [String] The search query.
124:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
125:     def web_search(query:)
126:       ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]), type: :web_search)
127:     end
128: 
129:     private
130: 
131:     def headers
132:       lock do
133:         (@headers || {}).merge(
134:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
135:           "x-api-key" => @key,
136:           "anthropic-version" => "2023-06-01",
137:           "anthropic-beta" => "files-api-2025-04-14"
138:         )
139:       end
140:     end
141: 
142:     def stream_parser
143:       LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser
144:     end
145: 
146:     def error_handler
147:       LLM::Anthropic::ErrorHandler
148:     end
149: 
150:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
151:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 1024}.merge!(params)
152:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
153:       params = [params, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
154:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
155:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
156:       [params, stream, tools, role]
157:     end
158: 
159:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
160:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
161:       payload = adapt(messages)
162:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload.merge!(params))
163:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/messages", headers)
164:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
165:       req
166:     end
167: 
168:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
169:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
170:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
171:       else
172:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
173:       end
174:     end
175:   end
176: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Google class implements a provider for
6:   # [Gemini](https://ai.google.dev/). The Google provider
7:   # can accept multiple inputs (text, images, audio, and video).
8:   # The inputs can be provided inline via the prompt for files
9:   # under 20MB or via the Gemini Files API for files
10:   # that are over 20MB.
11:   #
12:   # @example
13:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
14:   #   require "llm"
15:   #
16:   #   llm = LLM.google(key: ENV["KEY"])
17:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
18:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
19:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
20:   class Google < Provider
21:     require_relative "google/utils"
22:     require_relative "google/error_handler"
23:     require_relative "google/request_adapter"
24:     require_relative "google/response_adapter"
25:     require_relative "google/stream_parser"
26:     require_relative "google/models"
27:     require_relative "google/images"
28:     require_relative "google/audio"
29:     require_relative "google/files"
30: 
31:     include RequestAdapter
32:     extend Utils
33: 
34:     HOST = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
38:     def initialize(**)
39:       super(host: HOST, **)
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # @return [Symbol]
44:     #  Returns the provider's name
45:     def name
46:       :google
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # Provides an embedding
51:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
52:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
53:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
54:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
55:     # @return [LLM::Response]
56:     def embed(input, model: "gemini-embedding-001", **params)
57:       model = model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
58:       path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", "embedContent?key=#{@key}"].join(":")
59:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
60:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({content: {parts: [{text: input}]}})
61:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
62:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
63:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
64:       res
65:     end
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
69:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content#v1beta.models.generateContent Gemini docs
70:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
71:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
72:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
73:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
74:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
75:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
76:     # @return [LLM::Response]
77:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
78:       params, stream, tools, role, model = normalize_complete_params(params)
79:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
80:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model:)
81:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
82:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
83:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model:, res:, span:)
84:       res
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's audio API
89:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/audio Gemini docs
90:     # @return [LLM::Google::Audio]
91:     def audio
92:       LLM::Google::Audio.new(self)
93:     end
94: 
95:     ##
96:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's image generation API
97:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-generation Gemini docs
98:     # @return [see LLM::Google::Images]
99:     def images
100:       LLM::Google::Images.new(self)
101:     end
102: 
103:     ##
104:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's file management API
105:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/files Gemini docs
106:     # @return [LLM::Google::Files]
107:     def files
108:       LLM::Google::Files.new(self)
109:     end
110: 
111:     ##
112:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's models API
113:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models Gemini docs
114:     # @return [LLM::Google::Models]
115:     def models
116:       LLM::Google::Models.new(self)
117:     end
118: 
119:     ##
120:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
121:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models#gemini-2.5-flash gemini-2.5-flash
122:     # @return [String]
123:     def default_model
124:       "gemini-2.5-flash"
125:     end
126: 
127:     ##
128:     # @note
129:     #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
130:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
131:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
132:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/google-search Gemini docs
133:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
134:     def server_tools
135:       {
136:         google_search: server_tool(:google_search),
137:         code_execution: server_tool(:code_execution),
138:         url_context: server_tool(:url_context)
139:       }
140:     end
141: 
142:     ##
143:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
144:     # Google Search tool.
145:     # @param query [String] The search query.
146:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
147:     def web_search(query:)
148:       ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:google_search]]), type: :web_search)
149:     end
150: 
151:     ##
152:     # @return [Symbol]
153:     #  Returns the providers user role
154:     def user_role
155:       :user
156:     end
157: 
158:     ##
159:     # @return [Symbol]
160:     #  Returns the providers system role
161:     def system_role
162:       :user
163:     end
164: 
165:     ##
166:     # @return [Symbol]
167:     # Returns the providers developer role
168:     def developer_role
169:       :user
170:     end
171: 
172:     ##
173:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
174:     def assistant_role
175:       "model"
176:     end
177: 
178:     private
179: 
180:     def headers
181:       lock do
182:         (@headers || {}).merge(
183:           "Content-Type" => "application/json"
184:         )
185:       end
186:     end
187: 
188:     def stream_parser
189:       LLM::Google::StreamParser
190:     end
191: 
192:     def error_handler
193:       LLM::Google::ErrorHandler
194:     end
195: 
196:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
197:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
198:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
199:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
200:       role, model, stream = [:role, :model, :stream].map { params.delete(_1) }
201:       [params, stream, tools, role, model]
202:     end
203: 
204:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
205:       action = stream ? "streamGenerateContent?key=#{@key}&alt=sse" : "generateContent?key=#{@key}"
206:       model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
207:       path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", action].join(":")
208:       req  = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
209:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
210:       body = LLM.json.dump({contents: adapt(messages)}.merge!(params))
211:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
212:       req
213:     end
214: 
215:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
216:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
217:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
218:       else
219:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
220:       end
221:     end
222:   end
223: end
