1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Ollama class implements a provider for [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) –
6:   # and the provider supports a wide range of models. It is straight forward
7:   # to run on your own hardware, and there are a number of multi-modal models
8:   # that can process both images and text.
9:   #
10:   # @example
11:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
12:   #   require "llm"
13:   #
14:   #   llm = LLM.ollama(key: nil)
15:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, model: "llava")
16:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this image", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
17:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
18:   class Ollama < Provider
19:     require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
20:     require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
21:     require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
22:     require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
23:     require_relative "ollama/models"
24: 
25:     include RequestAdapter
26: 
27:     HOST = "localhost"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :ollama
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an embedding
44:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
45:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
47:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
48:     # @return [LLM::Response]
49:     def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
50:       params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
51:       req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
52:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
53:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
54:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
55:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
56:       res
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
61:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-a-chat-completion Ollama docs
62:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
63:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
64:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
65:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
66:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
67:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
68:     # @return [LLM::Response]
69:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
70:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
71:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
72:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
73:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
74:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
75:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
76:       res
77:     end
78: 
79:     ##
80:     # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
81:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
82:     # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
83:     def models
84:       LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
89:     def assistant_role
90:       "assistant"
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://ollama.com/library/qwen3 qwen3
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "qwen3:latest"
99:     end
100: 
101:     private
102: 
103:     def headers
104:       lock do
105:         (@headers || {}).merge(
106:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
107:           "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
108:         )
109:       end
110:     end
111: 
112:     def stream_parser
113:       LLM::Ollama::StreamParser
114:     end
115: 
116:     def error_handler
117:       LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler
118:     end
119: 
120:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
121:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
122:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
123:       params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
124:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
125:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
126:       [params, stream, tools, role]
127:     end
128: 
129:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
130:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
131:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
132:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
133:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
134:       req
135:     end
136: 
137:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
138:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
139:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
140:       else
141:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
142:       end
143:     end
144:   end
145: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require_relative "openai" unless defined?(LLM::OpenAI)
4: 
5: module LLM
6:   ##
7:   # The DeepSeek class implements a provider for
8:   # [DeepSeek](https://deepseek.com)
9:   # through its OpenAI-compatible API available via
10:   # their [web platform](https://platform.deepseek.com).
11:   #
12:   # @example
13:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
6:   # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
7:   #
8:   # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
9:   # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
10:   # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
11:   # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
12:   #
13:   # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
14:   # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
15:   # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
16:   # instead of a single API key.
17:   #
18:   # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
19:   # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
20:   # Ruby's stdlib.
21:   #
22:   # @example
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
26:   #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
27:   #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
28:   #     region: "us-east-1"
29:   #   )
30:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
31:   #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
32:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }
33:   class Bedrock < Provider
34:     require_relative "bedrock/signature"
35:     require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"
36:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter"
37:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter/completion"
38:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter"
39:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/completion"
40:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/models"
41:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_decoder"
42:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_parser"
43:     require_relative "bedrock/models"
44: 
45:     include RequestAdapter
46: 
47:     HOST_PATTERN = "bedrock-runtime.%s.amazonaws.com"
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @param [String] access_key_id AWS access key ID
51:     # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key
52:     # @param [String] region AWS region (e.g. "us-east-1")
53:     # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
54:     # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
55:     # @param [Integer] port Connection port
56:     # @param [Boolean] ssl Whether to use SSL
57:     # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds
58:     def initialize(access_key_id: nil, secret_access_key: nil,
59:                    region: nil, session_token: nil,
60:                    host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,
61:                    **)
62:       region ||= "us-east-1"
63:       @access_key_id = access_key_id
64:       @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
65:       @aws_region = region
66:       @session_token = session_token
67:       host ||= HOST_PATTERN % region
68:       @aws_host = host
69:       super(key: @access_key_id, host:, port:, ssl:, timeout:, persistent: false)
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @return [Symbol] Returns the provider's name
74:     def name
75:       :bedrock
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # Provides an interface to the Bedrock Converse API
80:     #
81:     # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_runtime_Converse.html
82:     #
83:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
84:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
85:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
86:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
87:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
88:       req, messages, body = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream:)
89:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
90:       sign!(req, body)
91:       model_id = model_id_for(req.path)
92:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, operation: "chat", stream_parser:, model: model_id)
93:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
94:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
95:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: model_id, res:, span:)
96:       res
97:     end
98: 
99:     ##
100:     # Provides an interface to Bedrock's ListFoundationModels API.
101:     #
102:     # @note
103:     #  Unlike the Converse API (bedrock-runtime), this endpoint lives
104:     #  on the control plane (bedrock.<region>.amazonaws.com).
105:     #
106:     # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_ListFoundationModels.html
107:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::Models]
108:     def models
109:       LLM::Bedrock::Models.new(self)
110:     end
111: 
112:     ##
113:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
114:     def files
115:       raise NotImplementedError
116:     end
117: 
118:     ##
119:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
120:     def images
121:       raise NotImplementedError
122:     end
123: 
124:     ##
125:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
126:     def audio
127:       raise NotImplementedError
128:     end
129: 
130:     ##
131:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
132:     def moderations
133:       raise NotImplementedError
134:     end
135: 
136:     ##
137:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
138:     def responses
139:       raise NotImplementedError
140:     end
141: 
142:     ##
143:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
144:     def vector_stores
145:       raise NotImplementedError
146:     end
147: 
148:     ##
149:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
150:     def embed(input, model: nil, **params)
151:       raise NotImplementedError
152:     end
153: 
154:     ##
155:     # @return [String]
156:     def assistant_role
157:       "assistant"
158:     end
159: 
160:     ##
161:     # Bedrock expects tool results as user messages containing
162:     # `toolResult` content blocks rather than a distinct `tool` role.
163:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
164:     def tool_role
165:       :user
166:     end
167: 
168:     ##
169:     # @return [String]
170:     def default_model
171:       "deepseek.v3.2"
172:     end
173: 
174:     private
175: 
176:     def headers
177:       lock do
178:         (@headers || {}).merge("Content-Type" => "application/json")
179:       end
180:     end
181: 
182:     def credentials
183:       LLM::Object.from(
184:         access_key_id: @access_key_id,
185:         secret_access_key: @secret_access_key,
186:         aws_region: @aws_region,
187:         host: @aws_host,
188:         session_token: @session_token
189:       )
190:     end
191: 
192:     def stream_parser
193:       LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser
194:     end
195: 
196:     def error_handler
197:       LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler
198:     end
199: 
200:     def stream_decoder
201:       LLM::Bedrock::StreamDecoder
202:     end
203: 
204:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
205:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 2048}.merge!(params)
206:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
207:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
208:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
209:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
210:       [params, stream, tools, role]
211:     end
212: 
213:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream: nil)
214:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
215:       model_id = params.delete(:model) || default_model
216:       payload = build_converse_payload(messages, params)
217:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload)
218:       path = stream ? "/model/#{model_id}/converse-stream" \
219:                     : "/model/#{model_id}/converse"
220:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
221:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
222:       [req, messages, body]
223:     end
224: 
225:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
226:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
227:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
228:       else
229:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
230:       end
231:     end
232: 
233:     def build_converse_payload(messages, params)
234:       adapted = adapt(messages)
235:       payload = {}
236:       payload[:system] = adapted[:system] if adapted[:system]&.any?
237:       payload[:messages] = adapted[:messages]
238:       inference_config = {}
239:       inference_config[:maxTokens] = params.delete(:max_tokens) if params[:max_tokens]
240:       inference_config[:temperature] = params.delete(:temperature) if params.key?(:temperature)
241:       inference_config[:topP] = params.delete(:top_p) if params.key?(:top_p)
242:       inference_config[:stopSequences] = params.delete(:stop) if params[:stop]
243:       payload[:inferenceConfig] = inference_config unless inference_config.empty?
244:       payload[:toolConfig] = params.delete(:toolConfig) if params[:toolConfig]
245:       payload[:outputConfig] = params.delete(:outputConfig) if params[:outputConfig]
246:       additional = {}
247:       top_k = params.delete(:top_k)
248:       additional[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
249:       payload[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional unless additional.empty?
250:       payload
251:     end
252: 
253:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
254:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
255:       value = message&.content || fallback
256:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
257:     end
258: 
259:     def model_id_for(path)
260:       path[%r{\A/model/(.+?)/converse(?:-stream)?\z}, 1] || default_model
261:     end
262: 
263:     def sign!(req, body)
264:       Signature.new(
265:         credentials:,
266:         method: req.method,
267:         path: req.path,
268:         body:
269:       ).sign!(req)
270:     end
271:   end
272: end

(End of file - total 272 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8:     require_relative "request_adapter/respond"
9:     require_relative "request_adapter/moderation"
10: 
11:     ##
12:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
13:     #  The messages to adapt
14:     # @param [Symbol] mode
15:     #  The mode to adapt the messages for
16:     # @return [Array<Hash>]
17:     def adapt(messages, mode: :complete)
18:       messages.filter_map do |message|
19:         if mode == :complete
20:           Completion.new(message).adapt
21:         else
22:           Respond.new(message).adapt
23:         end
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     private
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param [Hash] params
31:     # @return [Hash]
32:     def adapt_schema(params)
33:       return {} unless params and params[:schema]
34:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
35:       schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
36:       {
37:         response_format: {
38:           type: "json_schema",
39:           json_schema: {name: "JSONSchema", schema:}
40:         }
41:       }
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # @param [Hash] params
46:     # @return [Hash]
47:     def adapt_tools(tools)
48:       if tools.nil? || tools.empty?
49:         {}
50:       else
51:         {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
52:       end
53:     end
54:   end
55: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::OpenAI::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the OpenAI chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         elsif message.tool_call?
22:           {role: message.role, content: nil, tool_calls: message.extra[:original_tool_calls]}
23:         else
24:           adapt_message
25:         end
26:       end
27:     end
28: 
29:     private
30: 
31:     def adapt_message
32:       case content
33:       when Array
34:         adapt_array
35:       else
36:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def adapt_array
41:       if content.empty?
42:         nil
43:       elsif returns.any?
44:         returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
45:       else
46:         {role: message.role, content: content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }}
47:       end
48:     end
49: 
50:     def adapt_content(content)
51:       case content
52:       when LLM::Object
53:         adapt_object(content)
54:       when String
55:         [{type: :text, text: content.to_s}]
56:       when LLM::Response
57:         adapt_remote_file(content)
58:       when LLM::Message
59:         adapt_content(content.content)
60:       when LLM::Function::Return
61:         throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
62:       else
63:         prompt_error!(content)
64:       end
65:     end
66: 
67:     def adapt_object(object)
68:       case object.kind
69:       when :image_url
70:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: object.value}}]
71:       when :local_file
72:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
73:       when :remote_file
74:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
75:       else
76:         prompt_error!(object)
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def adapt_local_file(file)
81:       if file.image?
82:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: file.to_data_uri}}]
83:       else
84:         [{type: :file, file: {filename: file.basename, file_data: file.to_data_uri}}]
85:       end
86:     end
87: 
88:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
89:       if file.file?
90:         [{type: :file, file: {file_id: file.id}}]
91:       else
92:         prompt_error!(file)
93:       end
94:     end
95: 
96:     def prompt_error!(content)
97:       if LLM::Object === content
98:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
99:                                 "supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
100:       else
101:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
102:                                 "is not supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def message = @message
107:     def content = message.content
108:     def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
109:   end
110: end

20:     ##
21:     # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
22:     # @param [Symbol] type
23:     # @return [LLM::Response]
24:     def adapt(res, type:)
25:       response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
26:       adapter = select(type)
27:       response.extend(adapter)
28:     end

33:       case type
34:       when :audio then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Audio

36:       when :embedding then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Embedding

40:       when :moderations then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Moderations

42:       when :responds then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Responds
43:       when :web_search then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::WebSearch

12:           logprobs: choice.logprobs,
13:           reasoning_content: message.reasoning_content,
14:           tool_calls: adapt_tool_calls(message.tool_calls),
15:           original_tool_calls: message.tool_calls

17:         LLM::Message.new(message.role, message.content, extra)
18:       end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     EMPTY_HASH = {}.freeze
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
11:     # @return [Hash]
12:     attr_reader :body
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: {}}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
22:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
23:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
24:     end
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [Hash] chunk
28:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
29:     def parse!(chunk)
30:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
31:     end
32: 
33:     ##
34:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
35:     # @return [void]
36:     def free
37:       @emits.clear
38:     end
39: 
40:     private
41: 
42:     def merge!(chunk)
43:       chunk.each do |key, value|
44:         if key == "choices"
45:           @body["choices"] ||= []
46:           merge_choices!(value)
47:         else
48:           @body[key] = value
49:         end
50:       end
51:     end
52: 
53:     def merge_choices!(choices)
54:       body_choices = @body["choices"]
55:       choices.each do |choice|
56:         index = choice["index"]
57:         delta = choice["delta"] || EMPTY_HASH
58:         target_message = if body_choice = body_choices[index]
59:           body_choice["message"]
60:         else
61:           body_choices[index] = {"message" => {"role" => "assistant"}}
62:           body_choices[index]["message"]
63:         end
64:         merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
65:       end
66:     end
67: 
68:     def merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
69:       if delta.length == 1
70:         merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
71:       elsif content = delta["content"]
72:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
73:           target_content << content
74:         else
75:           target_message["content"] = content
76:         end
77:         emit_content(content)
78:       elsif reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
79:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
80:           target_reasoning << reasoning
81:         else
82:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
83:         end
84:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
85:       elsif tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
86:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
87:       end
88:       return if delta.length <= 1
89:       delta.each do |key, value|
90:         next if value.nil? || key == "content" || key == "reasoning_content" || key == "tool_calls"
91:         target_message[key] = value
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
96:       if content = delta["content"]
97:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
98:           target_content << content
99:         else
100:           target_message["content"] = content
101:         end
102:         emit_content(content)
103:         return
104:       end
105:       if reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
106:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
107:           target_reasoning << reasoning
108:         else
109:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
110:         end
111:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
112:         return
113:       end
114:       if tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
115:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     def merge_tools!(target, tools)
120:       target["tool_calls"] ||= []
121:       tools.each.with_index do |toola, index|
122:         tindex = toola["index"]
123:         tindex = index unless Integer === tindex && tindex >= 0
124:         toolb = target["tool_calls"][tindex]
125:         functiona = toola["function"]
126:         functionb = toolb && toolb["function"]
127:         if functiona && functionb
128:           # Append to existing function arguments
129:           merge_function!(functionb, functiona)
130:         else
131:           target["tool_calls"][tindex] = toola
132:         end
133:         emit_tool(target["tool_calls"][tindex], tindex)
134:       end
135:     end
136: 
137:     def merge_function!(target, source)
138:       if arguments = source["arguments"]
139:         if target_arguments = target["arguments"]
140:           target_arguments << arguments
141:         else
142:           target["arguments"] = arguments
143:         end
144:       end
145:       if name = source["name"]
146:         if target_name = target["name"]
147:           target_name << name
148:         else
149:           target["name"] = name
150:         end
151:       end
152:       return if source.length <= 2
153:       source.each do |func_key, func_value|
154:         next if func_key == "arguments" || func_key == "name"
155:         target[func_key] ||= +""
156:         target[func_key] << func_value
157:       end
158:     end
159: 
160:     def emit_content(value)
161:       if @can_emit_content
162:         @stream.on_content(value)
163:       elsif @can_push_content
164:         @stream << value
165:       end
166:     end
167: 
168:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
169:       if @can_emit_reasoning_content
170:         @stream.on_reasoning_content(value)
171:       end
172:     end
173: 
174:     def emit_tool(tool, tindex)
175:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
176:       return if @emits[:tools][tindex]
177:       function = tool["function"]
178:       return unless function && tool["id"] && function["name"]
179:       return unless arguments_complete?(function["arguments"])
180:       arguments = parse_arguments(function["arguments"])
181:       return unless arguments
182:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
183:       @emits[:tools][tindex] = true
184:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
188:       registered = @stream.__find__(function["name"])
189:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(function["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
190:         fn.id = tool["id"]
191:         fn.arguments = arguments
192:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
193:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
194:       end
195:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
196:     end
197: 
198:     def parse_arguments(arguments)
199:       return nil if arguments.to_s.empty?
200:       parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
201:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : nil
202:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
203:       nil
204:     end
205: 
206:     def arguments_complete?(arguments)
207:       value = arguments.to_s.rstrip
208:       !value.empty? && value.end_with?("}")
209:     end
210:   end
211: end

(End of file - total 211 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.unauthorized?
55:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
56:       elsif res.rate_limited?
57:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
58:       elsif res.not_found?
59:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
60:       else
61:         error = body["error"] || {}
62:         case error["type"]
63:         when "invalid_request_error" then handle_invalid_request(error)
64:         when "server_error"
65:           LLM::ServerError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
66:         else
67:           LLM::Error.new(error["message"] || "Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @param [Exception] error
74:     # @return [LLM::Error]
75:     def handle_invalid_request(error)
76:       case error["code"]
77:       when "context_length_exceeded"
78:         LLM::ContextWindowError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
79:       else
80:         LLM::InvalidRequestError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
86:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
87:     def parse_body!
88:       if String === res.body
89:         LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
90:       elsif Hash === res.body
91:         LLM::Object.from(res.body)
92:       else
93:         res.body
94:       end
95:     rescue
96:       res.body
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The `LLM::Contract` module provides the ability for modules
6:   # who are extended by it to implement contracts which must be
7:   # implemented by other modules who include a given contract.
8:   #
9:   # @example
10:   #   module LLM::Contract
11:   #      # ..
12:   #   end
13:   #
14:   #   module LLM::Contract
15:   #     module Completion
16:   #       extend LLM::Contract
17:   #       # inheriting modules must implement these methods
18:   #       # otherwise an error is raised on include
19:   #       def foo = nil
20:   #       def bar = nil
21:   #     end
22:   #   end
23:   #
24:   #   module LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter
25:   #     module Completion
26:   #       def foo = nil
27:   #       def bar = nil
28:   #       include LLM::Contract::Completion
29:   #     end
30:   #   end
31:   module Contract
32:     ContractError = Class.new(LLM::Error)
33:     require_relative "contract/completion"
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @api private
37:     def included(mod)
38:       meths = mod.instance_methods(false)
39:       if meths.empty?
40:         raise ContractError, "#{mod} does not implement any methods required by #{self}"
41:       end
42:       missing = instance_methods - meths
43:       if missing.any?
44:         raise ContractError, "#{mod} does not implement methods (#{missing.join(", ")}) required by #{self}"
45:       end
46:     end
47:   end
48: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
11:     #  The messages to adapt
12:     # @return [Hash]
13:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
14:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
15:       messages.each do |message|
16:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
17:         next if adapted.nil?
18:         if system?(message)
19:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
20:         else
21:           payload[:messages] << adapted
22:         end
23:       end
24:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
25:       payload
26:     end
27: 
28:     private
29: 
30:     ##
31:     # @param [Hash] params
32:     # @return [Hash]
33:     def adapt_tools(tools)
34:       return {} unless tools&.any?
35:       {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
36:     end
37: 
38:     def system?(message)
39:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
40:         message.system?
41:       else
42:         Hash === message and message[:role].to_s == "system"
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end
46: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Anthropic::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the Anthropic chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         else
22:           adapt_message
23:         end
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     private
28: 
29:     def adapt_message
30:       if message.tool_call?
31:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_tool_calls}
32:       else
33:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
34:       end
35:     end
36: 
37:     def adapt_tool_calls
38:       message.extra[:tool_calls].filter_map do |tool|
39:         next unless tool[:id] && tool[:name]
40:         {type: "tool_use", id: tool[:id], name: tool[:name], input: LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool[:arguments])}
41:       end
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # @param [String, URI] content
46:     #  The content to format
47:     # @return [String, Hash]
48:     #  The formatted content
49:     def adapt_content(content)
50:       case content
51:       when Hash
52:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : [content]
53:       when Array
54:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
55:       when LLM::Object
56:         adapt_object(content)
57:       when String
58:         [{type: :text, text: content}]
59:       when LLM::Response
60:         adapt_remote_file(content)
61:       when LLM::Message
62:         adapt_content(content.content)
63:       when LLM::Function::Return
64:         [{type: "tool_result", tool_use_id: content.id, content: [{type: :text, text: LLM.json.dump(content.value)}]}]
65:       else
66:         prompt_error!(content)
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     def adapt_object(object)
71:       case object.kind
72:       when :image_url
73:         [{type: :image, source: {type: "url", url: object.value.to_s}}]
74:       when :local_file
75:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
76:       when :remote_file
77:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
78:       else
79:         prompt_error!(content)
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def adapt_local_file(file)
84:       if file.image?
85:         [{type: :image, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
86:       elsif file.pdf?
87:         [{type: :document, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
88:       else
89:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{file.class}) " \
90:                                 "is not an image or PDF, and therefore not supported by the " \
91:                                 "Anthropic API"
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
96:       prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
97:       [{type: file.file_type, source: {type: :file, file_id: file.id}}]
98:     end
99: 
100:     def prompt_error!(content)
101:       if LLM::Object === content
102:       else
103:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
104:                                 "is not supported by the Anthropic API."
105:       end
106:     end
107: 
108:     def message = @message
109:     def content = message.content
110:   end
111: end

100:       source.map.with_index do |choice, index|

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
20:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
21:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @param [Hash] chunk
26:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
27:     def parse!(chunk)
28:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
33:     # @return [void]
34:     def free
35:     end
36: 
37:     private
38: 
39:     def merge!(chunk)
40:       if chunk["type"] == "message_start"
41:         merge_message!(chunk["message"])
42:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_start"
43:         @body["content"][chunk["index"]] = chunk["content_block"]
44:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_delta"
45:         if chunk["delta"]["type"] == "text_delta"
46:           @body["content"][chunk["index"]]["text"] << chunk["delta"]["text"]
47:           emit_content(chunk["delta"]["text"])
48:         elsif chunk["delta"]["type"] == "input_json_delta"
49:           content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
50:           if Hash === content["input"]
51:             content["input"] = chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
52:           else
53:             content["input"] ||= +""
54:             content["input"] << chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
55:           end
56:         end
57:       elsif chunk["type"] == "message_delta"
58:         merge_message!(chunk["delta"]) if chunk["delta"]
59:         extras = chunk.reject { |k, _| k == "type" || k == "delta" }
60:         merge_message!(extras) unless extras.empty?
61:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_stop"
62:         content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
63:         if content["input"]
64:           content["input"] = LLM.json.load(content["input"])
65:         end
66:         if content["type"] == "tool_use"
67:           emit_tool(content)
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_message!(message)
73:       message.each_pair do |key, value|
74:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair)
75:           @body[key] ||= {}
76:           deep_merge!(@body[key], value)
77:         else
78:           @body[key] = value
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def deep_merge!(target, source)
84:       source.each_pair do |key, value|
85:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair) && target[key].respond_to?(:each_pair)
86:           deep_merge!(target[key], value)
87:         else
88:           target[key] = value
89:         end
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     def emit_content(value)
94:       if @can_emit_content
95:         @stream.on_content(value)
96:       elsif @can_push_content
97:         @stream << value
98:       end
99:     end
100: 
101:     def emit_tool(tool)
102:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
103:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
104:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
105:     end
106: 
107:     def resolve_tool(tool)
108:       registered = @stream.__find__(tool["name"])
109:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(tool["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
110:         fn.id = tool["id"]
111:         fn.arguments = LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool["input"])
112:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
113:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
114:       end
115:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

(End of file - total 118 lines)
</content>

3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
11:     #  The messages to adapt
12:     # @return [Array<Hash>]
13:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
14:       messages.filter_map do |message|
15:         Completion.new(message).adapt
16:       end
17:     end
18: 
19:     private
20: 
21:     ##
22:     # @param [Hash] params
23:     # @return [Hash]
24:     def adapt_schema(params)
25:       return {} unless params and params[:schema]
26:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
27:       schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
28:       {generationConfig: {response_mime_type: "application/json", response_schema: schema}}
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @param [Hash] params
33:     # @return [Hash]
34:     def adapt_tools(tools)
35:       return {} unless tools&.any?
36:       platform, functions = [tools.grep(LLM::ServerTool), tools.grep(LLM::Function)]
37:       {tools: [*platform, {functionDeclarations: functions.map { _1.adapt(self) }}]}
38:     end
39:   end
40: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Google::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the Gemini chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], parts: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         elsif message.tool_call?
22:           {role: message.role, parts: message.extra.original_tool_calls}
23:         else
24:           {role: message.role, parts: adapt_content(message.content)}
25:         end
26:       end
27:     end
28: 
29:     def adapt_content(content)
30:       case content
31:       when Array
32:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
33:       when String
34:         [{text: content}]
35:       when LLM::Response
36:         adapt_remote_file(content)
37:       when LLM::Message
38:         adapt_content(content.content)
39:       when LLM::Function::Return
40:         [{functionResponse: {name: content.name, response: adapt_function_response(content.value)}}]
41:       when LLM::Object
42:         adapt_object(content)
43:       else
44:         prompt_error!(content)
45:       end
46:     end
47: 
48:     def adapt_object(object)
49:       case object.kind
50:       when :image_url
51:         [{file_data: {file_uri: object.value.to_s}}]
52:       when :local_file
53:         file = object.value
54:         [{inline_data: {mime_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
55:       when :remote_file
56:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
57:       else
58:         prompt_error!(object)
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
63:       return prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
64:       [{file_data: {mime_type: file.mime_type, file_uri: file.uri}}]
65:     end
66: 
67:     def adapt_function_response(value)
68:       Hash === value ? value : {result: value}
69:     end
70: 
71:     def prompt_error!(object)
72:       if LLM::Object === object
73:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
74:                                 "supported by the Gemini API"
75:       else
76:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
77:                                 "is not supported by the Gemini API"
78:       end
79:     end
80: 
81:     def message = @message
82:     def content = message.content
83:   end
84: end

2: 
3: module LLM::Google::ResponseAdapter
4:   module Completion
5:     ##
6:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#messages)
7:     def messages
8:       adapt_choices
9:     end
10:     alias_method :choices, :messages
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#id)
14:     def id
15:       body["responseId"]
16:     end
17: 
18:     ##
19:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_tokens)
20:     def input_tokens
21:       body.usageMetadata.promptTokenCount || 0
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_tokens)
26:     def output_tokens
27:       body.usageMetadata.candidatesTokenCount || 0
28:     end
29: 
30:     ##
31:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_tokens)
32:     def reasoning_tokens
33:       body.usageMetadata.thoughtsTokenCount || 0
34:     end
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_audio_tokens)
38:     def input_audio_tokens
39:       super
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_audio_tokens)
44:     def output_audio_tokens
45:       super
46:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"candidates" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: []}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
22:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
23:     end
24: 
25:     ##
26:     # @param [Hash] chunk
27:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
28:     def parse!(chunk)
29:       tap { merge_chunk!(chunk) }
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
34:     # @return [void]
35:     def free
36:       @emits.clear
37:     end
38: 
39:     private
40: 
41:     def merge_chunk!(chunk)
42:       chunk.each do |key, value|
43:         k = key.to_s
44:         if k == "candidates"
45:           merge_candidates!(value)
46:         elsif k == "usageMetadata" &&
47:             @body["usageMetadata"].is_a?(Hash) &&
48:             value.is_a?(Hash)
49:           @body["usageMetadata"] = @body["usageMetadata"].merge(value)
50:         else
51:           @body[k] = value
52:         end
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:     def merge_candidates!(deltas)
57:       deltas.each do |delta|
58:         index = delta["index"].to_i
59:         @body["candidates"][index] ||= {"content" => {"parts" => []}}
60:         candidate = @body["candidates"][index]
61:         delta.each do |key, value|
62:           k = key.to_s
63:           if k == "content"
64:             merge_candidate_content!(candidate["content"], value, index) if value
65:           else
66:             candidate[k] = value # Overwrite other fields
67:           end
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_candidate_content!(content, delta, cindex)
73:       delta.each do |key, value|
74:         k = key.to_s
75:         if k == "parts"
76:           content["parts"] ||= []
77:           merge_content_parts!(content["parts"], value, cindex) if value
78:         else
79:           content[k] = value
80:         end
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def merge_content_parts!(parts, deltas, cindex)
85:       deltas.each do |delta|
86:         if delta["text"]
87:           merge_text!(parts, delta)
88:         elsif delta["functionCall"]
89:           merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
90:         elsif delta["inlineData"]
91:           parts << delta
92:         elsif delta["functionResponse"]
93:           parts << delta
94:         elsif delta["fileData"]
95:           parts << delta
96:         else
97:           parts << delta
98:         end
99:       end
100:     end
101: 
102:     def merge_text!(parts, delta)
103:       last_existing_part = parts.last
104:       text = delta["text"]
105:       if last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) && last_existing_part["text"]
106:         last_existing_part["text"] ||= +""
107:         last_existing_part["text"] << text
108:         emit_content(text)
109:       else
110:         parts << delta
111:         emit_content(text)
112:       end
113:     end
114: 
115:     def merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
116:       last_existing_part = parts.last
117:       last_call = last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) ? last_existing_part["functionCall"] : nil
118:       delta_call = delta["functionCall"]
119:       if last_call.is_a?(Hash) && delta_call.is_a?(Hash)
120:         last_existing_part["functionCall"] = last_call.merge(delta_call)
121:         delta.each do |key, value|
122:           next if key == "functionCall"
123:           last_existing_part[key] = value
124:         end
125:       else
126:         parts << delta
127:       end
128:       emit_tool(parts.length - 1, cindex, parts.last || delta)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def emit_content(value)
132:       if @can_emit_content
133:         @stream.on_content(value)
134:       elsif @can_push_content
135:         @stream << value
136:       end
137:     end
138: 
139:     def emit_tool(pindex, cindex, part)
140:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
141:       return unless complete_tool?(part)
142:       key = [cindex, pindex]
143:       return if @emits[:tools].include?(key)
144:       function, error = resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
145:       @emits[:tools] << key
146:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def complete_tool?(part)
150:       call = part["functionCall"]
151:       call && call["name"] && Hash === call["args"]
152:     end
153: 
154:     def resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
155:       call = part["functionCall"]
156:       registered = @stream.__find__(call["name"])
157:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(call["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
158:         fn.id = LLM::Google.tool_id(part:, cindex:, pindex:)
159:         fn.arguments = call["args"]
160:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
161:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
162:       end
163:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
164:     end
165:   end
166: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Ollama::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the Ollama chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role]}.merge(adapt_content(message[:content]))
21:         else
22:           adapt_message
23:         end
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     private
28: 
29:     def adapt_content(content)
30:       case content
31:       when String
32:         {content:}
33:       when LLM::Message
34:         adapt_content(content.content)
35:       when LLM::Function::Return
36:         throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
37:       when LLM::Object
38:         adapt_object(content)
39:       else
40:         prompt_error!(content)
41:       end
42:     end
43: 
44:     def adapt_message
45:       case content
46:       when Array
47:         adapt_array
48:       else
49:         {role: message.role}.merge(adapt_content(content))
50:       end
51:     end
52: 
53:     def adapt_array
54:       if content.empty?
55:         nil
56:       elsif returns.any?
57:         returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
58:       else
59:         content.flat_map { {role: message.role}.merge(adapt_content(_1)) }
60:       end
61:     end
62: 
63:     def adapt_object(object)
64:       case object.kind
65:       when :local_file then adapt_local_file(object.value)
66:       when :remote_file then prompt_error!(object)
67:       when :image_url then prompt_error!(object)
68:       else prompt_error!(object)
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def adapt_local_file(file)
73:       if file.image?
74:         {content: "This message has an image associated with it", images: [file.to_b64]}
75:       else
76:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given local file (an instance of #{file.class}) " \
77:                                 "is not an image, and therefore not supported by the " \
78:                                 "Ollama API"
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     def prompt_error!(object)
83:       if LLM::Object === object
84:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
85:                                 "supported by the Ollama API"
86:       else
87:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
88:                                 "is not supported by the Ollama API"
89:       end
90:     end
91: 
92:     def message = @message
93:     def content = message.content
94:     def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
95:   end
96: end

3: class LLM::Ollama
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module ResponseAdapter
7:     require_relative "response_adapter/completion"
8:     require_relative "response_adapter/embedding"
9:     require_relative "response_adapter/models"
10: 
11:     module_function
12: 
13:     ##
14:     # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
15:     # @param [Symbol] type
16:     # @return [LLM::Response]
17:     def adapt(res, type:)
18:       response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
19:       response.extend(select(type))
20:     end
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # @api private
24:     def select(type)
25:       case type
26:       when :completion then LLM::Ollama::ResponseAdapter::Completion
27:       when :embedding then LLM::Ollama::ResponseAdapter::Embedding
28:       when :models then LLM::Ollama::ResponseAdapter::Models
29:       else
30:         raise ArgumentError, "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
31:       end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Ollama
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
14:     def initialize(stream)
15:       @body = {}
16:       @stream = stream
17:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
18:     end
19: 
20:     ##
21:     # @param [Hash] chunk
22:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
23:     def parse!(chunk)
24:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
25:     end
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
29:     # @return [void]
30:     def free
31:     end
32: 
33:     private
34: 
35:     def merge!(chunk)
36:       chunk.each do |key, value|
37:         if key == "message"
38:           if @body[key]
39:             @body[key]["content"] << value["content"]
40:             @stream << value["content"] if @can_push_content
41:           else
42:             @body[key] = value
43:             @stream << value["content"] if @can_push_content
44:           end
45:         else
46:           @body[key] = value
47:         end
48:       end
49:     end
50:   end
51: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Ollama
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [LLM::Error]
45:     def error
46:       if res.server_error?
47:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
48:       elsif res.unauthorized?
49:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
50:       elsif res.rate_limited?
51:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
52:       elsif res.not_found?
53:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       else
55:         LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
56:       end
57:     end
58:   end
59: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Adapts llm.rb internal message format to Bedrock Converse API format.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock Converse uses:
8:   #   - system: [{text: "..."}]  (top-level, separate from messages)
9:   #   - messages: [{role: "user"|"assistant", content: [{...}, ...]}]
10:   #   - Content blocks: text, image, document, toolUse, toolResult
11:   #   - toolConfig: {tools: [{toolSpec: {name:, description:, inputSchema: {json: ...}}}]}
12:   #
13:   # @api private
14:   module RequestAdapter
15:     ##
16:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
17:     # @return [Hash]
18:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
19:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
20:       messages.each do |message|
21:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
22:         next if adapted.nil?
23:         if system?(message)
24:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
25:         else
26:           payload[:messages] << adapted
27:         end
28:       end
29:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
30:       payload
31:     end
32: 
33:     private
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @param [Hash] params
37:     # @return [Hash]
38:     def adapt_schema(params)
39:       return {} unless params&.key?(:schema)
40:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
41:       schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
42:       cleaned = schema.respond_to?(:to_h) ? schema.to_h : schema
43:       [:strict, "strict", :$schema, "$schema"].each { cleaned.delete(_1) }
44:       {
45:         outputConfig: {
46:           textFormat: {
47:             type: "json_schema",
48:             structure: {
49:               jsonSchema: {
50:                 name: "response",
51:                 schema: LLM.json.dump(cleaned)
52:               }
53:             }
54:           }
55:         }
56:       }
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] tools
61:     # @return [Hash]
62:     def adapt_tools(tools)
63:       return {} unless tools&.any?
64:       {toolConfig: {tools: tools.map { |t| adapt_tool(t) }}}
65:     end
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # @param [LLM::Function] tool
69:     # @return [Hash]
70:     def adapt_tool(tool)
71:       function = tool.respond_to?(:function) ? tool.function : tool
72:       {
73:         toolSpec: {
74:           name: function.name,
75:           description: function.description,
76:           inputSchema: {
77:             json: function.params || default_input_schema
78:           }
79:         }
80:       }
81:     end
82: 
83:     def default_input_schema
84:       {"type" => "object", "properties" => {}, "required" => []}
85:     end
86: 
87:     def system?(message)
88:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
89:         message.system?
90:       else
91:         Hash === message && message[:role].to_s == "system"
92:       end
93:     end
94:   end
95: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Bedrock::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # Adapts a single message to Bedrock Converse content blocks.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock Converse content blocks include:
8:   #   - {text: "..."}
9:   #   - {image: {format: "png", source: {bytes: "..."}}}
10:   #   - {document: {format: "pdf", name: "...", source: {bytes: "..."}}}
11:   #   - {toolUse: {toolUseId: "...", name: "...", input: {...}}}
12:   #   - {toolResult: {toolUseId: "...", content: [{text: "..."}]}}
13:   #
14:   # @api private
15:   class Completion
16:     ##
17:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
18:     def initialize(message)
19:       @message = message
20:     end
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # Adapts the message for the Bedrock Converse API
24:     # @return [Hash, nil]
25:     def adapt
26:       catch(:abort) do
27:         if Hash === message
28:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
29:         else
30:           adapt_message
31:         end
32:       end
33:     end
34: 
35:     private
36: 
37:     def adapt_message
38:       if message.tool_call?
39:         blocks = [*adapt_tool_calls]
40:         blocks.unshift(*adapt_content(content)) unless String === content && content.empty?
41:         {role: "assistant", content: blocks}
42:       else
43:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
44:       end
45:     end
46: 
47:     def adapt_tool_calls
48:       message.extra[:tool_calls].filter_map do |tool|
49:         next unless tool[:id] && tool[:name]
50:         {
51:           toolUse: {
52:             toolUseId: tool[:id],
53:             name: tool[:name],
54:             input: parse_tool_input(tool[:arguments])
55:           }
56:         }
57:       end
58:     end
59: 
60:     ##
61:     # @param [String, Array, LLM::Object, LLM::Function::Return] content
62:     # @return [Array<Hash>, nil]
63:     def adapt_content(content)
64:       case content
65:       when Hash
66:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : [content]
67:       when Array
68:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
69:       when LLM::Object
70:         adapt_object(content)
71:       when String
72:         [{text: content}]
73:       when LLM::Response
74:         adapt_remote_file(content)
75:       when LLM::Message
76:         adapt_content(content.content)
77:       when LLM::Function::Return
78:         [{toolResult: {toolUseId: content.id, content: [{text: LLM.json.dump(content.value)}]}}]
79:       else
80:         prompt_error!(content)
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def adapt_object(object)
85:       case object.kind
86:       when :image_url
87:         [{image: {format: detect_format(object.value.to_s),
88:                   source: {url: object.value.to_s}}}]
89:       when :local_file
90:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
91:       when :remote_file
92:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
93:       else
94:         prompt_error!(object)
95:       end
96:     end
97: 
98:     def adapt_local_file(file)
99:       if file.image?
100:         [{image: {format: file.format,
101:                   source: {bytes: file.to_b64}}}]
102:       elsif file.pdf?
103:         name = sanitize_name(file.basename)
104:         [{document: {format: "pdf", name:,
105:                      source: {bytes: file.to_b64}}}]
106:       else
107:         raise LLM::PromptError,
108:               "The #{file.class} is not an image or PDF, " \
109:               "and not supported by the Bedrock API"
110:       end
111:     end
112: 
113:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
114:       prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
115:       [{file.file_type => {source: {file_id: file.id}}}]
116:     end
117: 
118:     def detect_format(url)
119:       case url
120:       when /\.png/i then "png"
121:       when /\.jpe?g/i then "jpeg"
122:       when /\.gif/i then "gif"
123:       when /\.webp/i then "webp"
124:       else "png"
125:       end
126:     end
127: 
128:     def sanitize_name(name)
129:       name.to_s.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/, "_")
130:     end
131: 
132:     def parse_tool_input(input)
133:       case input
134:       when Hash then input
135:       when String
136:         parsed = LLM.json.load(input)
137:         Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
138:       when nil then {}
139:       else input.respond_to?(:to_h) ? input.to_h : {}
140:       end
141:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
142:       {}
143:     end
144: 
145:     def prompt_error!(content)
146:       raise LLM::PromptError,
147:             "#{content.class} is not supported by the Bedrock API"
148:     end
149: 
150:     def message = @message
151:     def content = message.content
152:   end
153: end

(End of file - total 153 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Adapts Bedrock Converse API responses to llm.rb's Response format.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock Converse returns:
8:   #   {
9:   #     output: {message: {role: "assistant", content: [{...}, ...]}},
10:   #     usage: {inputTokens: N, outputTokens: N},
11:   #     modelId: "anthropic.claude-...",
12:   #     stopReason: "end_turn" | "tool_use" | "max_tokens" | ...
13:   #   }
14:   #
15:   # @api private
16:   module ResponseAdapter
17:     module_function
18: 
19:     ##
20:     # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
21:     # @param [Symbol] type
22:     # @return [LLM::Response]
23:     def adapt(res, type:)
24:       response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
25:       response.extend(select(type))
26:     end
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @api private
30:     def select(type)
31:       case type
32:       when :completion then LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter::Completion
33:       when :models then LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter::Models
34:       else
35:         raise ArgumentError,
36:               "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
37:       end
38:     end
39:   end
40: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # Adapts Bedrock Converse API completion responses.
6:   #
7:   # The Bedrock Converse response body looks like:
8:   #   {
9:   #     "output" => {"message" => {
10:   #       "role" => "assistant",
11:   #       "content" => [{"text" => "..."}, {"toolUse" => {...}}]
12:   #     }},
13:   #     "usage" => {"inputTokens" => N, "outputTokens" => N},
14:   #     "modelId" => "anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0",
15:   #     "stopReason" => "end_turn"
16:   #   }
17:   module Completion
18:     ##
19:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#messages)
20:     def messages
21:       source = texts.empty? && tools.any? ? [{"text" => ""}] : texts
22:       source.map.with_index do |choice, index|
23:         extra = {
24:           index:, response: self,
25:           reasoning_content:,
26:           tool_calls: adapt_tool_calls(tools),
27:           original_tool_calls: tools
28:         }
29:         LLM::Message.new(role, choice["text"], extra)
30:       end
31:     end
32:     alias_method :choices, :messages
33: 
34:     ##
35:     # Returns the Bedrock request id when present.
36:     # @return [String, nil]
37:     def id
38:       res["x-amzn-requestid"] || res["x-amzn-request-id"]
39:     end
40: 
41:     ##
42:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_tokens)
43:     def input_tokens
44:       body.usage&.inputTokens || 0
45:     end
46: 
47:     ##
48:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_tokens)
49:     def output_tokens
50:       body.usage&.outputTokens || 0
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_tokens)
55:     def reasoning_tokens
56:       0
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_audio_tokens)
61:     def input_audio_tokens
62:       super
63:     end
64: 
65:     ##
66:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_audio_tokens)
67:     def output_audio_tokens
68:       super
69:     end
70: 
71:     ##
72:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_image_tokens)
73:     def input_image_tokens
74:       super
75:     end
76: 
77:     ##
78:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_read_tokens)
79:     def cache_read_tokens
80:       0
81:     end
82: 
83:     ##
84:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_write_tokens)
85:     def cache_write_tokens
86:       0
87:     end
88: 
89:     ##
90:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#total_tokens)
91:     def total_tokens
92:       input_tokens + output_tokens
93:     end
94: 
95:     ##
96:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#usage)
97:     def usage
98:       super
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#model)
103:     def model
104:       body.modelId
105:     end
106: 
107:     ##
108:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content)
109:     def content
110:       super
111:     end
112: 
113:     ##
114:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_content)
115:     def reasoning_content
116:       @reasoning_content ||= begin
117:         text = parts.filter_map { _1.dig("reasoningContent", "text") }.join
118:         text.empty? ? nil : text
119:       end
120:     end
121: 
122:     ##
123:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content!)
124:     def content!
125:       super
126:     end
127: 
128:     private
129: 
130:     def adapt_tool_calls(tools)
131:       (tools || []).filter_map do |tool|
132:         next unless tool["toolUse"]
133:         {
134:           id: tool["toolUse"]["toolUseId"],
135:           name: tool["toolUse"]["name"],
136:           arguments: parse_tool_input(tool["toolUse"]["input"])
137:         }
138:       end
139:     end
140: 
141:     def parse_tool_input(input)
142:       case input
143:       when Hash then input
144:       when String
145:         parsed = LLM.json.load(input)
146:         Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
147:       when nil then {}
148:       else input.respond_to?(:to_h) ? input.to_h : {}
149:       end
150:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
151:       {}
152:     end
153: 
154:     def parts
155:       raw = body.output&.message&.content || []
156:       raw.is_a?(Array) ? raw : [raw].compact
157:     end
158: 
159:     def texts
160:       @texts ||= parts.select { |b| b["text"] }
161:     end
162: 
163:     def tools
164:       @tools ||= parts.select { |b| b["toolUse"] }
165:     end
166: 
167:     def role
168:       body.output&.message&.role || "assistant"
169:     end
170: 
171:     include LLM::Contract::Completion
172:   end
173: end
