1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Parses Bedrock Converse Stream events into a response body
6:   # and emits stream callbacks (on_content, on_tool_call, etc.).
7:   #
8:   # Receives decoded JSON payloads from {StreamDecoder} along with
9:   # the AWS Event Stream event type header.
10:   #
11:   # Bedrock Converse Stream event types:
12:   #   messageStart — initial role
13:   #   contentBlockStart — tool use or reasoning start
14:   #   contentBlockDelta — text delta, tool input JSON, or reasoning text
15:   #   contentBlockStop — content block finished
16:   #   messageStop — final stop reason, usage metadata
17:   #
18:   # @api private
19:   class StreamParser
20:     TOOL_MARKER = "<｜DSML｜function_calls"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # @return [Hash] Fully constructed response body
24:     attr_reader :body
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
28:     def initialize(stream)
29:       @body = {"output" => {"message" => {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}}}
30:       @stream = stream
31:       @text_markers = {}
32:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
33:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
34:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
35:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
36:     end
37: 
38:     ##
39:     # @param [Hash] payload Decoded JSON from an event stream frame
40:     # @param [String, nil] event_type The :event-type header value
41:     # @return [self]
42:     def parse!(payload, event_type: nil)
43:       type = event_type || payload["type"]
44:       case type
45:       when "messageStart"
46:         # { "role" => "assistant" }
47:       when "contentBlockStart"
48:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "start" => { "toolUse" => {...} } }
49:         handle_content_block_start(payload)
50:       when "contentBlockDelta"
51:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "delta" => { "text" => "..." } }
52:         handle_content_block_delta(payload)
53:       when "contentBlockStop"
54:         handle_content_block_stop(payload)
55:       when "messageStop"
56:         # { "stopReason" => "end_turn", "metadata" => {"usage" => {...}} }
57:         merge_metadata(payload)
58:       when "metadata"
59:         # { "usage" => {...} }
60:         merge_metadata(payload)
61:       end
62:       self
63:     end
64: 
65:     ##
66:     # @return [void]
67:     def free
68:       @text_markers.clear
69:     end
70: 
71:     private
72: 
73:     def handle_content_block_start(payload)
74:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
75:       start_data = payload["start"] || {}
76:       if (tool_use = start_data["toolUse"])
77:         content[index] = {"toolUse" => {"toolUseId" => tool_use["toolUseId"], "name" => tool_use["name"], "input" => +""}}
78:       elsif (reasoning = start_data["reasoningContent"])
79:         content[index] = {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +"", "signature" => reasoning["signature"]}.compact}
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def handle_content_block_delta(payload)
84:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
85:       delta = payload["delta"] || {}
86:       if (text = delta["text"])
87:         ensure_content_block(index, "text")
88:         visible = filtered_text(index, text)
89:         return if visible.empty?
90:         content[index]["text"] ||= +""
91:         content[index]["text"] << visible
92:         emit_content(visible)
93:       elsif (tool_input = delta.dig("toolUse", "input"))
94:         ensure_content_block(index, "tool_use")
95:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] ||= +""
96:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] << tool_input
97:       elsif (reasoning = delta["reasoningContent"])
98:         ensure_content_block(index, "reasoning")
99:         if reasoning["text"]
100:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] ||= +""
101:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] << reasoning["text"]
102:           emit_reasoning_content(reasoning["text"])
103:         end
104:         if reasoning["signature"]
105:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["signature"] = reasoning["signature"]
106:         end
107:       end
108:     end
109: 
110:     def handle_content_block_stop(payload)
111:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
112:       item = content[index]
113:       return unless item
114:       flush_text(index, item)
115:       if item["toolUse"] && item["toolUse"]["input"].is_a?(String)
116:         parsed = LLM.json.load(item["toolUse"]["input"])
117:         item["toolUse"]["input"] = parsed.is_a?(Hash) ? parsed : {}
118:         emit_tool(item)
119:       end
120:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
121:       item["toolUse"]["input"] = {} if item&.dig("toolUse")
122:     end
123: 
124:     def ensure_content_block(index, type)
125:       content[index] ||= case type
126:       when "tool_use" then {"toolUse" => {"input" => +""}}
127:       when "reasoning" then {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +""}}
128:       else {}
129:       end
130:     end
131: 
132:     def filtered_text(index, text)
133:       state = (@text_markers[index] ||= +"")
134:       value = state << text
135:       value.gsub!(TOOL_MARKER, "")
136:       keep = marker_prefix_length(value)
137:       @text_markers[index] = keep.zero? ? +"" : value[-keep..]
138:       keep.zero? ? value : value[0...-keep]
139:     end
140: 
141:     def flush_text(index, item)
142:       value = @text_markers.delete(index).to_s
143:       return unless item["text"]
144:       if value.empty?
145:         content[index] = {} if item["text"].empty?
146:       else
147:         item["text"] << value
148:         emit_content(value)
149:       end
150:     end
151: 
152:     def marker_prefix_length(value)
153:       [value.length, TOOL_MARKER.length - 1].min.downto(1) do |length|
154:         return length if TOOL_MARKER.start_with?(value[-length..])
155:       end
156:       0
157:     end
158: 
159:     def merge_metadata(payload)
160:       metadata = payload["metadata"] || payload
161:       return unless metadata.is_a?(Hash)
162:       usage = metadata["usage"]
163:       @body["usage"] = usage if usage
164:       @body["stopReason"] = payload["stopReason"] if payload["stopReason"]
165:     end
166: 
167:     def emit_content(value)
168:       if @can_emit_content
169:         @stream.on_content(value)
170:       elsif @can_push_content
171:         @stream << value
172:       end
173:     end
174: 
175:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
176:       @stream.on_reasoning_content(value) if @can_emit_reasoning_content
177:     end
178: 
179:     def emit_tool(tool)
180:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
181:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
182:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
183:     end
184: 
185:     def resolve_tool(tool)
186:       payload = tool["toolUse"] || {}
187:       registered = @stream.__find__(payload["name"])
188:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(payload["name"])).dup.tap do |f|
189:         f.id = payload["toolUseId"]
190:         f.arguments = payload["input"] || {}
191:         f.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
192:         f.model = @stream.extra[:model]
193:       end
194:       [fn, registered ? nil : fn.unavailable]
195:     end
196: 
197:     def content
198:       @body["output"]["message"]["content"]
199:     end
200:   end
201: end

(End of file - total 201 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "digest"
4: 
5: class LLM::Bedrock
6:   ##
7:   # Decodes AWS Event Stream binary frames.
8:   #
9:   # Bedrock Converse Stream uses the AWS Event Stream protocol,
10:   # a binary framing format (not SSE). Each message has:
11:   #   - total length (4 bytes, big-endian)
12:   #   - headers length (4 bytes, big-endian)
13:   #   - prelude CRC (4 bytes)
14:   #   - headers (variable)
15:   #   - payload (variable, usually JSON)
16:   #   - message CRC (4 bytes)
17:   #
18:   # Implements #<< to match the interface expected by llm.rb's
19:   # streaming transport, so it can replace the SSE-based
20:   # StreamDecoder when streaming from Bedrock.
21:   #
22:   # @api private
23:   class StreamDecoder
24:     ##
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser]
26:     attr_reader :parser
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @param [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser] parser
30:     def initialize(parser)
31:       @buffer = +"".b
32:       @parser = parser
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # Feeds a raw binary chunk into the decoder.
37:     # Accumulates data until complete frames are available,
38:     # then decodes them and passes the JSON payload to the parser.
39:     #
40:     # @param [String] chunk Raw binary data
41:     # @return [void]
42:     def <<(chunk)
43:       @buffer << chunk
44:       decode_frames
45:     end
46: 
47:     ##
48:     # @return [Hash] The fully constructed response body
49:     def body
50:       parser.body
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # @return [void]
55:     def free
56:       @buffer.clear
57:       parser.free if parser.respond_to?(:free)
58:     end
59: 
60:     private
61: 
62:     def decode_frames
63:       loop do
64:         break if @buffer.bytesize < 12
65:         total_length = @buffer[0, 4].unpack1("N")
66:         break if @buffer.bytesize < total_length
67:         # headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
68:         # prelude_crc = @buffer[8, 4].unpack1("N")
69:         headers = decode_headers
70:         payload_start = 12 + headers[:length]
71:         payload_length = total_length - payload_start - 4
72:         payload = @buffer[payload_start, payload_length] if payload_length > 0
73:         # message_crc from last 4 bytes, not needed for our purposes
74:         json = payload ? LLM.json.load(payload) : {}
75:         parser.parse!(json, event_type: headers[:event_type]) if json.is_a?(Hash)
76:         @buffer = @buffer[total_length..] || +"".b
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def decode_headers
81:       headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
82:       offset = 12
83:       end_offset = offset + headers_length
84:       result = {event_type: nil, length: headers_length}
85:       while offset < end_offset
86:         name_len = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
87:         offset += 1
88:         break if offset + name_len > end_offset
89:         name = @buffer[offset, name_len]
90:         offset += name_len
91:         break if offset >= end_offset
92:         value_type = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
93:         offset += 1
94:         value = case value_type
95:         when 7 # string
96:           str_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
97:           offset += 2
98:           str = @buffer[offset, str_len]
99:           offset += str_len
100:           str
101:         when 8 # binary
102:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
103:           offset += 2
104:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
105:           offset += bin_len
106:           bin
107:         when 9 # boolean true
108:           true
109:         when 1 # boolean false
110:           false
111:         when 2 # byte
112:           val = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
113:           offset += 1
114:           val
115:         when 3 # int16
116:           val = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("s>")
117:           offset += 2
118:           val
119:         when 4 # int32
120:           val = @buffer[offset, 4].unpack1("l>")
121:           offset += 4
122:           val
123:         when 6 # byte array (as raw string)
124:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
125:           offset += 2
126:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
127:           offset += bin_len
128:           bin
129:         else
130:           # Unknown type, skip to end of headers
131:           offset = end_offset
132:           nil
133:         end
134:         result[:event_type] = value if name == ":event-type"
135:         result[name] = value if name
136:       end
137:       result
138:     end
139:   end
140: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Handles Bedrock API error responses.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock errors come as JSON with:
8:   #   { "message" => "...", "__type" => "..." }
9:   # or as standard HTTP status codes.
10:   #
11:   # @api private
12:   class ErrorHandler
13:     ##
14:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
15:     attr_reader :res
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @return [Object, nil]
19:     attr_reader :span
20: 
21:     ##
22:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
23:     # @param [Object, nil] span
24:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler]
26:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
27:       @tracer = tracer
28:       @span = span
29:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [LLM::Error]
45:     def error
46:       message = extract_message
47:       if res.server_error?
48:         LLM::ServerError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
49:       elsif res.unauthorized?
50:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
51:       elsif res.forbidden?
52:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
53:       elsif res.rate_limited?
54:         LLM::RateLimitError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
55:       elsif res.not_found?
56:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
57:       else
58:         LLM::Error.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # @return [String]
64:     def extract_message
65:       body = parse_body
66:       body["message"] || body["Message"] || body["__type"] || "Unexpected error"
67:     end
68: 
69:     ##
70:     # @return [Hash]
71:     def parse_body
72:       return {} if res.body.nil? || res.body.empty?
73:       parsed = LLM.json.load(res.body.dup.force_encoding(Encoding::UTF_8).scrub)
74:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
75:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
76:       {}
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::DeepSeek::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the DeepSeek chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         elsif message.tool_call?
22:           wrap(content: nil, tool_calls: message.extra[:original_tool_calls])
23:         else
24:           adapt_message
25:         end
26:       end
27:     end
28: 
29:     private
30: 
31:     def adapt_content(content)
32:       case content
33:       when LLM::Object
34:         adapt_object(content)
35:       when String
36:         [{type: :text, text: content.to_s}]
37:       when LLM::Message
38:         adapt_content(content.content)
39:       when LLM::Function::Return
40:         throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
41:       else
42:         prompt_error!(content)
43:       end
44:     end
45: 
46:     def adapt_object(object)
47:       case object.kind
48:       when :image_url, :local_file, :remote_file
49:         prompt_error!(object)
50:       else
51:         prompt_error!(object)
52:       end
53:     end
54: 
55:     def adapt_message
56:       case content
57:       when Array
58:         adapt_array
59:       else
60:         wrap(content: adapt_content(content))
61:       end
62:     end
63: 
64:     def adapt_array
65:       if content.empty?
66:         nil
67:       elsif returns.any?
68:         returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
69:       else
70:         wrap(content: content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) })
71:       end
72:     end
73: 
74:     def prompt_error!(object)
75:       if LLM::Object === object
76:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{object.kind}' is not " \
77:                                 "supported by the DeepSeek API"
78:       else
79:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
80:                                 "is not supported by the DeepSeek API"
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def wrap(content:, tool_calls: nil)
85:       {
86:         role: message.role,
87:         content:,
88:         tool_calls: tool_calls&.map { LLM::Object === _1 ? _1.to_h : _1 },
89:         reasoning_content: message.reasoning_content
90:       }.compact.then { preserve_nil_content(_1) }
91:     end
92: 
93:     def message = @message
94:     def content = message.content
95:     def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
96: 
97:     def preserve_nil_content(hash)
98:       hash[:content] = content if content.nil?
99:       hash
100:     end
101:   end
102: end

(End of file - total 102 lines)

73:       allow(http).to receive(:finish)

76:       provider.interrupt!(owner)

87:       provider = LLM.openai(key: "test", transport:)
88:       client = persistent_class.allocate

90:       allow(client).to receive(:finish)
91:       req = LLM::Transport::PersistentHTTP::ActiveRequest.new(client:, connection:)
92:       provider.send(:transport).send(:set_request, req, owner)
93:       provider.interrupt!(owner)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::OpenAI::Models LLM::OpenAI::Models} class provides a model
6:   # object for interacting with [OpenAI's models API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/models/list).
7:   # The models API allows a client to query OpenAI for a list of models
8:   # that are available for use with the OpenAI API.
9:   #
10:   # @example
11:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
12:   #   require "llm"
13:   #
14:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
15:   #   res = llm.models.all
16:   #   res.each do |model|
17:   #     print "id: ", model.id, "\n"
18:   #   end
19:   class Models
20:     ##
21:     # Returns a new Models object
22:     # @param provider [LLM::Provider]
23:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Files]
24:     def initialize(provider)
25:       @provider = provider
26:     end
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # List all models
30:     # @example
31:     #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
32:     #   res = llm.models.all
33:     #   res.each do |model|
34:     #     print "id: ", model.id, "\n"
35:     #   end
36:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/models/list OpenAI docs
37:     # @param [Hash] params Other parameters (see OpenAI docs)
38:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
39:     # @return [LLM::Response]
40:     def all(**params)
41:       query = URI.encode_www_form(params)
42:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.get(path("/models?#{query}"), headers)
43:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "request")
44:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :models)
45:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "request", res:, span:)
46:       res
47:     end
48: 
49:     private
50: 
51:     [:path, :headers, :execute].each do |m|
52:       define_method(m) { |*args, **kwargs, &b| @provider.send(m, *args, **kwargs, &b) }
53:     end
54:   end
55: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder}
6:   # incrementally decodes streamed HTTP response bodies into parser
7:   # events.
8:   #
9:   # @api private
10:   class StreamDecoder
11:     ##
12:     # @return [Object]
13:     attr_reader :parser
14: 
15:     ##
16:     # @param [#parse!, #body, nil] parser
17:     # @yieldparam [Hash] chunk
18:     # @return [LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder]
19:     def initialize(parser = nil, &on_chunk)
20:       @buffer = +""
21:       @cursor = 0
22:       @data = []
23:       @parser = parser
24:       @on_chunk = on_chunk
25:     end
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [String] chunk
29:     # @return [void]
30:     def <<(chunk)
31:       @buffer << chunk
32:       each_line { handle_line(_1) }
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Object]
37:     def body
38:       parser.body
39:     end
40: 
41:     ##
42:     # @return [void]
43:     def free
44:       @buffer.clear
45:       @cursor = 0
46:       @data.clear
47:       parser.free if parser.respond_to?(:free)
48:     end
49: 
50:     private
51: 
52:     def handle_line(line)
53:       if line == "\n" || line == "\r\n"
54:         flush_sse_event
55:       elsif line.start_with?("data:")
56:         @data << field_value(line)
57:       elsif line.start_with?("event:", "id:", "retry:", ":")
58:       else
59:         decode!(strip_newline(line))
60:       end
61:     end
62: 
63:     def flush_sse_event
64:       return if @data.empty?
65:       decode!(@data.join("\n"))
66:       @data.clear
67:     end
68: 
69:     def field_value(line)
70:       value_start = line.getbyte(5) == 32 ? 6 : 5
71:       strip_newline(line.byteslice(value_start..))
72:     end
73: 
74:     def strip_newline(line)
75:       line = line.byteslice(0, line.bytesize - 1) if line.end_with?("\n")
76:       line = line.byteslice(0, line.bytesize - 1) if line.end_with?("\r")
77:       line
78:     end
79: 
80:     def decode!(payload)
81:       return if payload.empty? || payload == "[DONE]"
82:       chunk = LLM.json.load(payload)
83:       return unless chunk
84:       parser ? parser.parse!(chunk) : @on_chunk&.call(chunk)
85:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
86:     end
87: 
88:     def each_line
89:       while (newline = @buffer.index("\n", @cursor))
90:         line = @buffer[@cursor..newline]
91:         @cursor = newline + 1
92:         yield(line)
93:       end
94:       return if @cursor.zero?
95:       @buffer = @buffer[@cursor..] || +""
96:       @cursor = 0
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

(End of file - total 99 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::Stream LLM::Stream} class provides the callback interface for
6:   # streamed model output in llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # A stream object can be an instance of {LLM::Stream LLM::Stream} or a
9:   # subclass that overrides the callbacks it needs. For basic streaming,
10:   # llm.rb also accepts any object that implements `#<<`. {#queue} provides
11:   # a small helper for collecting asynchronous tool work started from a
12:   # callback.
13:   #
14:   # @note The `on_*` callbacks run inline with the streaming parser. They
15:   #   therefore block streaming progress and should generally return as
16:   #   quickly as possible.
17:   #
18:   # The most common callback is {#on_content}, which also maps to {#<<}.
19:   # Providers may also call {#on_reasoning_content} and {#on_tool_call} when
20:   # that data is available. Runtime features such as context compaction may
21:   # also emit lifecycle callbacks like {#on_transform} or {#on_compaction}.
22:   class Stream
23:     require_relative "stream/queue"
24: 
25:     ##
26:     # Returns extra context associated with the current streamed request.
27:     # @return [Hash]
28:     def extra
29:       @extra ||= LLM::Object.from({})
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Returns the current context, if one was attached to the stream.
34:     # @return [LLM::Context, nil]
35:     def ctx
36:       extra[:ctx]
37:     end
38: 
39:     ##
40:     # Returns a lazily-initialized queue for tool results or spawned work.
41:     # @return [LLM::Stream::Queue]
42:     def queue
43:       @queue ||= Queue.new(self)
44:     end
45: 
46:     ##
47:     # Waits for queued tool work to finish and returns function results.
48:     # Any passed arguments are ignored because queued work is waited according
49:     # to the actual task types already present in the queue.
50:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
51:     def wait(*)
52:       queue.wait
53:     end
54: 
55:     # @group Public callbacks
56: 
57:     ##
58:     # Called when visible assistant output is streamed.
59:     # @param [String] content
60:     #  A chunk of assistant-visible text.
61:     # @return [nil]
62:     def on_content(content)
63:       nil
64:     end
65:     alias_method :<<, :on_content
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # Called when reasoning output is streamed separately from visible content.
69:     # @param [String] content
70:     #  A chunk of reasoning text.
71:     # @return [nil]
72:     def on_reasoning_content(content)
73:       nil
74:     end
75: 
76:     ##
77:     # Called when a streamed tool call has been fully constructed.
78:     # @note A stream implementation may start tool execution here, for
79:     #   example by pushing `ctx.spawn(tool, :thread)`,
80:     #   `ctx.spawn(tool, :fiber)`, or `ctx.spawn(tool, :task)` onto {#queue}.
81:     #   Mixed strategies can also be selected per tool, such as
82:     #   `tool.mcp? ? ctx.spawn(tool, :task) : ctx.spawn(tool, :ractor)`.
83:     #   When a streamed tool cannot be resolved, `error` is passed as an
84:     #   {LLM::Function::Return}. It can be sent back to the model, allowing
85:     #   the tool-call path to recover and the session to continue. Streamed
86:     #   tool resolution now prefers the current request tools, so
87:     #   {LLM.function}, MCP tools, bound tool instances, and normal
88:     #   {LLM::Tool LLM::Tool} classes can all resolve through the same
89:     #   request-local path. The current `:ractor` mode is for class-based
90:     #   tools and does not support MCP tools.
91:     # @param [LLM::Function] tool
92:     #  The parsed tool call.
93:     # @param [LLM::Function::Return, nil] error
94:     #  An in-band tool error for unresolved tool calls.
95:     # @return [nil]
96:     def on_tool_call(tool, error)
97:       nil
98:     end
99: 
100:     ##
101:     # Called when queued streamed tool work returns.
102:     # @note This callback runs when {#wait} resolves work that was queued from
103:     #   {#on_tool_call}, such as values returned by `ctx.spawn(tool, :thread)`,
104:     #   `ctx.spawn(tool, :fiber)`, or `ctx.spawn(tool, :task)`.
105:     # @param [LLM::Function] tool
106:     #  The tool that returned.
107:     # @param [LLM::Function::Return] result
108:     #  The completed tool return.
109:     # @return [nil]
110:     def on_tool_return(tool, result)
111:       nil
112:     end
113: 
114:     ##
115:     # Called before a context transformer rewrites a prompt.
116:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
117:     # @param [#call] transformer
118:     # @return [nil]
119:     def on_transform(ctx, transformer)
120:       nil
121:     end
122: 
123:     ##
124:     # Called after a context transformer finishes rewriting a prompt.
125:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
126:     # @param [#call] transformer
127:     # @return [nil]
128:     def on_transform_finish(ctx, transformer)
129:       nil
130:     end
131: 
132:     ##
133:     # Called before a context compaction starts.
134:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
135:     # @param [LLM::Compactor] compactor
136:     # @return [nil]
137:     def on_compaction(ctx, compactor)
138:       nil
139:     end
140: 
141:     ##
142:     # Called after a context compaction finishes.
143:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
144:     # @param [LLM::Compactor] compactor
145:     # @return [nil]
146:     def on_compaction_finish(ctx, compactor)
147:       nil
148:     end
149: 
150:     # @endgroup
151: 
152:     # @group Finders
153: 
154:     ##
155:     # Resolves a streamed tool call against the current request tools first,
156:     # then falls back to the global function registry.
157:     # @param [String] name
158:     # @return [LLM::Function, nil]
159:     def __find__(name)
160:       tools = extra[:tools] || ctx&.params&.dig(:tools) || []
161:       tool = tools.find do
162:         candidate = _1.respond_to?(:function) ? _1.function.name : _1.name
163:         candidate.to_s == name.to_s
164:       end
165:       if tool
166:         tool.respond_to?(:function) ? tool.function : tool
167:       else
168:         LLM::Function.find_by_name(name)
169:       end
170:     end
171: 
172:     # @endgroup
173:   end
174: end

(End of file - total 174 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "bundler/gem_tasks"
4: require "rspec/core/rake_task"
5: 
6: cassettes = File.join(__dir__, "spec", "fixtures", "cassettes")
7: remotes = %w[openai google anthropic deepseek]
8: locals  = %w[ollama llamacpp]
9: bundler = ENV["bundler"] || "bundle"
10: 
11: desc "Run linter"
12: task :rubocop do
13:   sh "#{bundler} exec rubocop"
14: end
15: 
16: namespace :spec do
17:   namespace :remote do
18:     desc "Clear remote cassette cache"
19:     task :clear do
20:       remotes.each { rm_rf File.join(cassettes, _1) }
21:     end
22:   end
23: 
24:   desc "Run remote tests"
25:   task :remote do
26:     paths = ["spec/readme_spec.rb", "spec/{#{remotes.join(",")}}/**/*.rb"]
27:     specs = Dir[*paths].shuffle
28:     sh "#{bundler} exec rspec #{specs.join(' ')}"
29:   end
30: 
31:   namespace :local do
32:     desc "Clear local cassette cache"
33:     task :clear do
34:       locals.each { rm_rf File.join(cassettes, _1) }
35:     end
36:   end
37: end
38: 
39: desc "Run all tests"
40: task :spec do
41:   sh "#{bundler} exec rspec spec"
42: end
43: 
44: desc "Start a console with all providers loaded"
45: task :console do
46:   require "llm"
47:   require "dotenv"
48:   Dotenv.load
49:   openai = LLM.openai(key: ENV["OPENAI_SECRET"])
50:   google = LLM.google(key: ENV["GEMINI_SECRET"])
51:   anthropic = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["ANTHROPIC_SECRET"])
52:   deepseek = LLM.deepseek(key: ENV["DEEPSEEK_SECRET"])
53:   xai = LLM.xai(key: ENV["XAI_SECRET"])
54:   binding.irb
55: end
56: 
57: Dir[File.join(__dir__, "tasks", "*.rake")].sort.each do |task|
58:   load task
59: end
60: 
61: namespace :'models.dev' do
62:   desc "Download models.dev metadata"
63:   task :download do
64:     require "net/http"
65:     require "json"
66:     client = Net::HTTP.new "models.dev", 443
67:     client.use_ssl = true

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::Google::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.bad_request?
55:         reason = body.dig("error", "details", 0, "reason")
56:         if reason == "API_KEY_INVALID"
57:           LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
58:         else
59:           LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
60:         end
61:       elsif res.rate_limited?
62:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
63:       elsif res.not_found?
64:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
65:       else
66:         LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
72:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
73:     def parse_body!
74:       if String === res.body
75:         LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
76:       elsif Hash === res.body
77:         LLM::Object.from(res.body)
78:       else
79:         res.body
80:       end
81:     rescue
82:       res.body
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   module Utils
5:     ##
6:     # Returns a stable internal tool-call ID for Gemini function calls.
7:     #
8:     # Gemini responses may omit a direct tool-call ID, but llm.rb expects one
9:     # for matching pending tool calls with tool returns across streaming and
10:     # normal completion flows.
11:     #
12:     # When Gemini provides a `thoughtSignature`, that value is used as the
13:     # basis for the ID. Otherwise the ID falls back to the candidate and part
14:     # indexes, which are stable within the response.
15:     #
16:     # @param part [Hash]
17:     #   A Gemini content part containing a `functionCall`.
18:     # @param cindex [Integer]
19:     #   The candidate index for the tool call.
20:     # @param pindex [Integer]
21:     #   The part index for the tool call within the candidate.
22:     # @return [String]
23:     #   Returns a stable internal tool-call ID.
24:     def tool_id(part:, cindex:, pindex:)
25:       signature = part["thoughtSignature"].to_s
26:       return "google_#{signature}" unless signature.empty?
27:       "google_call_#{cindex}_#{pindex}"
28:     end
29:   end
30: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport::Response
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP}
6:   # adapts a {Net::HTTPResponse Net::HTTPResponse} to the
7:   # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} interface.
8:   #
9:   # This is the default wrapper for responses produced by the built-in
10:   # {LLM::Transport::HTTP LLM::Transport::HTTP} transport.
11:   class HTTP < self
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Net::HTTPResponse]
14:     attr_reader :res
15: 
16:     ##
17:     # @param [Net::HTTPResponse] res
18:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP]
19:     def initialize(res)
20:       @res = res
21:     end
22: 
23:     ##
24:     # @return [String]
25:     def code
26:       @res.code
27:     end
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @return [Object]
31:     def body
32:       @res.body
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @param [Object] value
37:     # @return [Object]
38:     def body=(value)
39:       @res.body = value
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # @param [String] key
44:     # @return [String, nil]
45:     def [](key)
46:       @res[key]
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @param [Object, nil] dest
51:     # @yieldparam [String] chunk
52:     # @return [void]
53:     def read_body(dest = nil, &block)
54:       if dest && block
55:         @res.read_body(dest) { block.call(_1) }
56:       elsif dest
57:         @res.read_body(dest)
58:       elsif block
59:         @res.read_body { block.call(_1) }
60:       else
61:         @res.read_body
62:       end
63:     end
64: 
65:     ##
66:     # @return [Boolean]
67:     def success?
68:       Net::HTTPSuccess === @res
69:     end
70: 
71:     ##
72:     # @return [Boolean]
73:     def ok?
74:       Net::HTTPOK === @res
75:     end
76: 
77:     ##
78:     # @return [Boolean]
79:     def bad_request?
80:       Net::HTTPBadRequest === @res
81:     end
82: 
83:     ##
84:     # @return [Boolean]
85:     def unauthorized?
86:       Net::HTTPUnauthorized === @res
87:     end
88: 
89:     ##
90:     # @return [Boolean]
91:     def forbidden?
92:       Net::HTTPForbidden === @res
93:     end
94: 
95:     ##
96:     # @return [Boolean]
97:     def not_found?
98:       Net::HTTPNotFound === @res
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # @return [Boolean]
103:     def rate_limited?
104:       Net::HTTPTooManyRequests === @res
105:     end
106: 
107:     ##
108:     # @return [Boolean]
109:     def server_error?
110:       Net::HTTPServerError === @res
111:     end
112:   end
113: end

3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::OpenAI::Responses LLM::OpenAI::Responses} class provides
6:   # an interface for [OpenAI's response API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/conversation-state?api-mode=responses).
7:   #
8:   # @example example #1
9:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
10:   #   require "llm"
11:   #
12:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
13:   #   res1 = llm.responses.create "Your task is to answer the user's questions", role: :developer
14:   #   res2 = llm.responses.create "5 + 5 = X ?", role: :user, previous_response_id: res1.id
15:   #   [res1, res2].each { llm.responses.delete(_1) }
16:   class Responses
17:     require_relative "responses/stream_parser"
18:     include RequestAdapter
19: 
20:     ##
21:     # Returns a new Responses object
22:     # @param provider [LLM::Provider]
23:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Responses]
24:     def initialize(provider)
25:       @provider = provider
26:     end
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # Create a response
30:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/create OpenAI docs
31:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
32:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
33:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
34:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
35:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
36:     # @return [LLM::Response]
37:     def create(prompt, params = {})
38:       params = {role: :user, model: @provider.default_model}.merge!(params)
39:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
40:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
41:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
42:       params[:stream] = true if @provider.streamable?(stream) || stream == true
43:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path("/responses"), headers)
44:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
45:       @provider.tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
46:       body = LLM.json.dump({input: [adapt(messages, mode: :response)].flatten}.merge!(params))
47:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
48:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, stream_parser:, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
49:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :responds)
50:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
51:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
52:       res
53:     end
54: 
55:     ##
56:     # Get a response
57:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/get OpenAI docs
58:     # @param [#id, #to_s] response Response ID
59:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
60:     # @return [LLM::Response]
61:     def get(response, **params)
62:       response_id = response.respond_to?(:id) ? response.id : response
63:       query = URI.encode_www_form(params)
64:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.get(path("/responses/#{response_id}?#{query}"), headers)
65:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "request")
66:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :responds)
67:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "request", res:, span:)
68:       res
69:     end
70: 
71:     ##
72:     # Deletes a response
73:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/delete OpenAI docs
74:     # @param [#id, #to_s] response Response ID
75:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
76:     # @return [LLM::Object] Response body
77:     def delete(response)
78:       response_id = response.respond_to?(:id) ? response.id : response
79:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.delete(path("/responses/#{response_id}"), headers)
80:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "request")
81:       res = LLM::Response.new(res)
82:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "request", res:, span:)
83:       res
84:     end
85: 
86:     private
87: 
88:     [:path, :headers, :execute, :transport, :resolve_tools].each do |m|
89:       define_method(m) { |*args, **kwargs, &b| @provider.send(m, *args, **kwargs, &b) }
90:     end
91: 
92:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
93:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
94:         [*(params.delete(:input) || []), *prompt]
95:       else
96:         [*(params.delete(:input) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     def adapt_schema(params)
101:       return {} unless params && params[:schema]
102:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
103:       schema = schema.to_h.merge(additionalProperties: false)
104:       name = "JSONSchema"
105:       {text: {format: {type: "json_schema", name:, schema:}}}
106:     end
107: 
108:     def stream_parser
109:       LLM::OpenAI::Responses::StreamParser
110:     end
111: 
112:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
113:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
114:       value = message&.content || fallback
115:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

(End of file - total 118 lines)

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/llm.rb/lib/llm/providers/openai/responses/stream_parser.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Responses::StreamParser
7:     EMPTY_HASH = {}.freeze
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
11:     # @return [Hash]
12:     attr_reader :body
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
16:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
17:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Responses::StreamParser]
18:     def initialize(stream)
19:       @body = {"output" => []}
20:       @stream = stream
21:       @emits = {tools: {}}
22:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
23:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
24:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
25:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
26:       @cached_output_index = nil
27:       @cached_output_item = nil
28:       @cached_content_index = nil
29:       @cached_content_part = nil
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # @param [Hash] chunk
34:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Responses::StreamParser]
35:     def parse!(chunk)
36:       tap { handle_event(chunk) }
37:     end
38: 
39:     ##
40:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
41:     # @return [void]
42:     def free
43:       @emits.clear
44:       clear_cache!
45:     end
46: 
47:     private
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @group Dispatchers
51: 
52:     def handle_event(chunk)
53:       output = @body["output"]
54:       type = chunk["type"]
55:       if type == "response.output_text.delta"
56:         merge_output_text_delta!(output, chunk)
57:       elsif type == "response.content_part.added"
58:         merge_content_part!(output, chunk)
59:       elsif type == "response.output_item.added"
60:         merge_output_item!(output, chunk)
61:       elsif type == "response.function_call_arguments.delta"
62:         merge_function_call_arguments_delta!(output, chunk)
63:       elsif type == "response.function_call_arguments.done"
64:         merge_function_call_arguments_done!(output, chunk)
65:       elsif type == "response.output_item.done"
66:         merge_output_item!(output, chunk)
67:       elsif type == "response.content_part.done"
68:         merge_content_part!(output, chunk, part_key: "part")
69:       else
70:         case type
71:         when "response.created"
72:           merge_response_created!(chunk)
73:         when "response.in_progress", "response.completed"
74:           merge_response_state!(output, chunk)
75:         when "response.reasoning_summary_text.delta"
76:           merge_reasoning_summary_text_delta!(output, chunk)
77:         when "response.reasoning_summary_text.done"
78:           merge_reasoning_summary_text_done!(output, chunk)
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     ##
84:     # @endgroup
85: 
86:     ##
87:     # @group Mergers
88: 
89:     def merge_response_created!(chunk)
90:       clear_cache!
91:       chunk.each do |k, v|
92:         next if k == "type"
93:         @body[k] = v
94:       end
95:       @body["output"] ||= []
96:     end
97: 
98:     def merge_response_state!(output, chunk)
99:       clear_cache!
100:       response = chunk["response"] || EMPTY_HASH
101:       response.each do |k, v|
102:         next if k == "output" && Array === output && output.any?
103:         @body[k] = v
104:       end
105:       @body["output"] ||= response["output"] || []
106:     end
107: 
108:     def merge_output_item!(output, chunk)
109:       output_index = chunk["output_index"]
110:       item = chunk["item"]
111:       output[output_index] = item
112:       item["content"] ||= [] if item["type"] == "message" || item.key?("content")
113:       item["summary"] ||= [] if item["type"] == "reasoning"
114:       cache_output_item!(output_index, item)
115:     end
116: 
117:     def merge_content_part!(output, chunk, part_key: "part")
118:       output_index = chunk["output_index"]
119:       content_index = chunk["content_index"]
120:       part = chunk[part_key]
121:       output_item = output_item_at(output, output_index)
122:       unless output_item
123:         output_item = {"content" => []}
124:         output[output_index] = output_item
125:         cache_output_item!(output_index, output_item)
126:       end
127:       content = output_item["content"] ||= []
128:       content[content_index] = part
129:       cache_content_part!(content_index, part)
130:     end
131: 
132:     def merge_output_text_delta!(output, chunk)
133:       content_part = content_part_at(output, chunk["output_index"], chunk["content_index"])
134:       if content_part && content_part["type"] == "output_text"
135:         delta_text = chunk["delta"]
136:         if text = content_part["text"]
137:           text << delta_text
138:         else
139:           content_part["text"] = delta_text
140:         end
141:         emit_content(delta_text)
142:       end
143:     end
144: 
145:     def merge_reasoning_summary_text_delta!(output, chunk)
146:       output_item = output_item_at(output, chunk["output_index"])
147:       if output_item && output_item["type"] == "reasoning"
148:         summary_index = chunk["summary_index"] || 0
149:         delta = chunk["delta"]
150:         summary = output_item["summary"] ||= []
151:         if summary_item = summary[summary_index]
152:           summary_item["text"] << delta
153:         else
154:           summary[summary_index] = {"type" => "summary_text", "text" => delta}
155:         end
156:         emit_reasoning_content(delta)
157:       end
158:     end
159: 
160:     def merge_reasoning_summary_text_done!(output, chunk)
161:       output_item = output_item_at(output, chunk["output_index"])
162:       if output_item && output_item["type"] == "reasoning"
163:         summary_index = chunk["summary_index"] || 0
164:         output_item["summary"] ||= []
165:         output_item["summary"][summary_index] = {
166:           "type" => "summary_text",
167:           "text" => chunk["text"]
168:         }
169:       end
170:     end
171: 
172:     def merge_function_call_arguments_delta!(output, chunk)
173:       output_item = output_item_at(output, chunk["output_index"])
174:       if output_item && output_item["type"] == "function_call"
175:         if arguments = output_item["arguments"]
176:           arguments << chunk["delta"]
177:         else
178:           output_item["arguments"] = chunk["delta"]
179:         end
180:       end
181:     end
182: 
183:     def merge_function_call_arguments_done!(output, chunk)
184:       output_item = output_item_at(output, chunk["output_index"])
185:       if output_item && output_item["type"] == "function_call"
186:         output_item["arguments"] = chunk["arguments"]
187:         emit_tool(chunk["output_index"], output_item)
188:       end
189:     end
190: 
191:     ##
192:     # @endgroup
193: 
194:     ##
195:     # @group Cache
196: 
197:     def output_item_at(output, output_index)
198:       if @cached_output_index == output_index
199:         @cached_output_item
200:       else
201:         cache_output_item!(output_index, output[output_index])
202:       end
203:     end
204: 
205:     def content_part_at(output, output_index, content_index)
206:       if @cached_output_index == output_index && @cached_content_index == content_index
207:         @cached_content_part
208:       else
209:         output_item = output_item_at(output, output_index)
210:         content = output_item && output_item["content"]
211:         cache_content_part!(content_index, content && content[content_index])
212:       end
213:     end
214: 
215:     def cache_output_item!(output_index, output_item)
216:       @cached_output_index = output_index
217:       @cached_output_item = output_item
218:       @cached_content_index = nil
219:       @cached_content_part = nil
220:       output_item
221:     end
222: 
223:     def cache_content_part!(content_index, content_part)
224:       @cached_content_index = content_index
225:       @cached_content_part = content_part
226:       content_part
227:     end
228: 
229:     def clear_cache!
230:       @cached_output_index = nil
231:       @cached_output_item = nil
232:       @cached_content_index = nil
233:       @cached_content_part = nil
234:     end
235: 
236:     ##
237:     # @endgroup
238: 
239:     ##
240:     # @group Emitters
241: 
242:     def emit_content(value)
243:       if @can_emit_content
244:         @stream.on_content(value)
245:       elsif @can_push_content
246:         @stream << value
247:       end
248:     end
249: 
250:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
251:       @stream.on_reasoning_content(value) if @can_emit_reasoning_content
252:     end
253: 
254:     def emit_tool(index, tool)
255:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
256:       return if @emits[:tools][index]
257:       return unless tool["call_id"] && tool["name"]
258:       arguments = parse_arguments(tool["arguments"])
259:       return unless arguments
260:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool, arguments)
261:       @emits[:tools][index] = true
262:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
263:     end
264: 
265:     ##
266:     # @endgroup
267: 
268:     ##
269:     # @group Resolvers
270: 
271:     def resolve_tool(tool, arguments)
272:       registered = @stream.__find__(tool["name"])
273:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(tool["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
274:         fn.id = tool["call_id"]
275:         fn.arguments = arguments
276:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
277:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
278:       end
279:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
280:     end
281: 
282:     def parse_arguments(arguments)
283:       return nil if arguments.to_s.empty?
284:       parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
285:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : nil
286:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
287:       nil
288:     end
289: 
290:     ##
291:     # @endgroup
292:   end
293: end

(End of file - total 293 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "setup"
4: 
5: RSpec.describe "LLM::OpenAI::Responses" do
6:   let(:key) { ENV["OPENAI_SECRET"] || "TOKEN" }
7:   let(:provider) { LLM.openai(key:) }
8: 
9:   context "when given a successful create operation",
10:           vcr: {cassette_name: "openai/responses/successful_create"} do
11:     subject { provider.responses.create("Hello", role: :developer) }
12: 
13:     it "is successful" do
14:       is_expected.to be_instance_of(LLM::Response)
15:     end
16: 
17:     it "has outputs" do
18:       is_expected.to have_attributes(
19:         choices: [instance_of(LLM::Message)]
20:       )
21:     end
22:   end
23: 
24:   context "when given a successful get operation",
25:           vcr: {cassette_name: "openai/responses/successful_get"} do
26:     let(:response) { provider.responses.create("Hello", role: :developer) }
27:     subject { provider.responses.get(response) }
28: 
29:     it "is successful" do
30:       is_expected.to be_instance_of(LLM::Response)
31:     end
32: 
33:     it "has outputs" do
34:       is_expected.to have_attributes(
35:         choices: [instance_of(LLM::Message)]
36:       )
37:     end
38:   end
39: 
40:   context "when given a successful delete operation",
41:           vcr: {cassette_name: "openai/responses/successful_delete"} do
42:     let(:response) { provider.responses.create("Hello", role: :developer) }
43:     subject { provider.responses.delete(response) }
44: 
45:     it "is successful" do
46:       is_expected.to have_attributes(
47:         deleted: true
48:       )
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   context "when given a json schema",
53:           vcr: {cassette_name: "openai/responses/json_schema"} do
54:     subject do
55:       schema = provider.schema.object(answer: provider.schema.string.required)
56:       provider.responses.create("What is the capital of France?", role: :user, schema:)
57:     end
58: 
59:     it "is successful" do
60:       is_expected.to be_instance_of(LLM::Response)
61:     end
62: 
63:     it "has outputs" do
64:       is_expected.to have_attributes(
65:         choices: [have_attributes(content: /Paris/)]
66:       )
67:     end
68: 
69:     it "has token usage" do
70:       is_expected.to have_attributes(
71:         prompt_tokens: be_a(Integer),
72:         completion_tokens: be_a(Integer),
73:         total_tokens: be_a(Integer)
74:       )
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   context "when given a function call",
79:           vcr: {cassette_name: "openai/responses/function_call"} do
80:     let(:ctx) { LLM::Context.new(provider, mode: :responses, tools: [tool]) }
81:     let(:tool) do
82:       LLM.function(:system) do |fn|
83:         fn.description "Runs system commands"
84:         fn.params { _1.object(command: _1.string.description("The command to run").required) }
85:         fn.define { |command:| {success: Kernel.system(command)} }
86:       end
87:     end
88:     let(:prompt) do
89:       ctx.build_prompt do
90:         _1.system "You are a bot that can run UNIX commands"
91:         _1.user "What is the date?"
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     before do
96:       allow(Kernel).to receive(:system).and_return("2024-01-01")
97:     end
98: 
99:     it "calls a function" do
100:       ctx.talk(prompt)

# llm.rb provider-addition map

## Step 1: Project orientation

llm.rb is a Ruby LLM client that exposes one normalized API over multiple providers. Code lives under `lib/llm/`, with provider integrations under `lib/llm/providers/<name>/`. Shared machinery is in the top-level `lib/llm/` files.

Key landmarks:

- Entry / factory methods: `lib/llm.rb:112-181`
- Shared provider base: `lib/llm/provider.rb`
- Transport layer: `lib/llm/transport.rb`, `lib/llm/transport/http.rb`, `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb`
- Normalized request/response: `lib/llm/transport/request.rb`, `lib/llm/transport/response.rb`
- Stateful chat boundary: `lib/llm/context.rb`
- Completion response contract: `lib/llm/contract/completion.rb`
- Model registry loader: `lib/llm/registry.rb`; registry data: `data/*.json`
- Existing providers: `lib/llm/providers/{openai,anthropic,google,ollama,bedrock,deepseek,xai,zai,llamacpp}.rb`

A caller obtains a provider through `LLM.<name>(...)`, e.g. `LLM.openai(key:)` at `lib/llm.rb:152-155`.

## Step 2: Chat/completion call trace

Trace for `LLM.openai(key:).chat("hi").talk("hello")`:

1. Factory: `LLM.openai` → `lib/llm.rb:152-155`
2. Provider init: `LLM::OpenAI#initialize` → `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:35-37`
3. Chat starts a context: `LLM::Provider#chat` → `lib/llm/provider.rb:103-106`
4. Context turn: `LLM::Context#talk` → `lib/llm/context.rb:193-204`
5. Context delegates to completions API: `LLM::Context#complete` → `lib/llm/context.rb:558-564`
6. Provider implementation: `LLM::OpenAI#complete` → `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:73-82`
7. Params normalized: `LLM::OpenAI#normalize_complete_params` → `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:210-220`
8. Request built via `RequestAdapter`: `build_complete_request` → `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:222-228`; per-message adapter `RequestAdapter::Completion#adapt` → `lib/llm/providers/openai/request_adapter/completion.rb:17-27`
9. Execution: `LLM::Transport::Execution#execute` → `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:31-42`
10. HTTP request: `LLM::Transport::HTTP#request` → `lib/llm/transport/http.rb:79-86`
11. Response handling: `LLM::Transport::Execution#handle_response` → `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:51-54`
12. Body parsed: `LLM::Transport::Execution#parse_response` → `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:60-65`
13. Adapted to library shape: `LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter.adapt` → `lib/llm/providers/openai/response_adapter.rb:24-28`; completion module → `lib/llm/providers/openai/response_adapter/completion.rb:7-19`
14. Wrapped as `LLM::Response` → `lib/llm/response.rb:19-83`

## Step 3: Provider base contract and customizations

`LLM::Provider` (`lib/llm/provider.rb`) declares the contract a subclass must implement:

- Identity: `name` → `lib/llm/provider.rb:55-57`
- Core ops: `embed` → `70-72`, `complete` → `94-96`
- Capability endpoints: `responses` → `126-128`, `images` → `133-135`, `audio` → `140-142`, `files` → `147-149`, `models` → `154-156`, `moderations` → `161-163`, `vector_stores` → `168-170`
- Role/model accessors: `assistant_role` → `176-178`, `default_model` → `183-185`
- Private hooks: `headers` → `362-364`, `error_handler` → `371-373`, `stream_parser` → `384-386`
- Shared defaults: `user_role` → `249-251`, `system_role` → `255-257`, `developer_role` → `261-263`, `tool_role` → `267-269`, `resolve_tools` → `402-412`, `streamable?` → `338-340`

`chat` and `respond` are implemented on the base class and create a `LLM::Context` (`lib/llm/provider.rb:103-117`).

Completion responses must satisfy `LLM::Contract::Completion` (`lib/llm/contract/completion.rb`):

- Required: `messages`/`choices`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `reasoning_tokens`, `total_tokens`, `model`
- Default-zero optional: `input_audio_tokens`, `output_audio_tokens`, `input_image_tokens`, `cache_read_tokens`, `cache_write_tokens`
- Convenience: `content`, `reasoning_content`, `content!`, `usage`

Provider customizations:

- `OpenAI`: full-featured; implements all endpoints including `embed`, `responses`, `images`, `audio`, `files`, `models`, `moderations`, `vector_stores`, `web_search` → `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb`
- `Anthropic`: `complete`, `models`, `files`, `web_search`; overrides `tool_role` to `:user` → `lib/llm/providers/anthropic.rb:89-91`
- `Google`: `complete`, `embed`, `models`, `images`, `audio`, `files`, `web_search`; overrides `user_role`/`system_role`/`developer_role` to `:user` and `assistant_role` to `"model"` → `lib/llm/providers/google.rb:152-176`
- `Ollama`: `complete`, `embed`, `models` → `lib/llm/providers/ollama.rb`
- `Bedrock`: `complete`, `models`; raises `NotImplementedError` for `files`, `images`, `audio`, `moderations`, `responses`, `vector_stores`, `embed` → `lib/llm/providers/bedrock.rb:114-152`
- `DeepSeek < OpenAI`: reuses OpenAI machinery; overrides `name`, `default_model`; raises for files/images/audio/moderations/responses/vector_stores; includes custom `RequestAdapter` → `lib/llm/providers/deepseek.rb`
- `XAI < OpenAI`: custom `images`; raises for other non-chat endpoints → `lib/llm/providers/xai.rb`
- `ZAI < OpenAI`: overrides host, `completions_path`, `default_model`; raises for non-chat endpoints → `lib/llm/providers/zai.rb`
- `LlamaCpp < OpenAI`: local defaults; raises for non-chat endpoints → `lib/llm/providers/llamacpp.rb`

## Step 4: Per-provider component families

Every provider owns a parallel component family:

| Component | Typical path | Tied together in provider via |
|---|---|---|
| Provider class | `lib/llm/providers/<name>.rb` | `class <Name> < Provider` |
| Error handler | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/error_handler.rb` | `error_handler` method returning `LLM::<Name>::ErrorHandler` |
| Request adapter module | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/request_adapter.rb` | `include RequestAdapter` |
| Request adapter completion | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/request_adapter/completion.rb` | Required by `request_adapter.rb` |
| Response adapter module | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/response_adapter.rb` | `ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: ...)` calls |
| Response adapter completion | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/response_adapter/completion.rb` | Selected by type `:completion` |
| Stream parser | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/stream_parser.rb` | `stream_parser` method returning parser class |
| Optional endpoint APIs | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/{models,files,images,audio,responses,vector_stores,moderations}.rb` | Provider method returning `LLM::<Name>::<Endpoint>.new(self)` |
| Optional response adapters | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/response_adapter/{embedding,models,file,image,web_search,audio,enumerable,files,responds}.rb` | Registered in `ResponseAdapter.select` |
| Optional utils | `lib/llm/providers/<name>/utils.rb` | `extend Utils` |

Examples:

- OpenAI ties components at `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:16-30`, response adapter registry at `lib/llm/providers/openai/response_adapter.rb:32-47`
- Anthropic ties components at `lib/llm/providers/anthropic.rb:16-27`, response adapter registry at `lib/llm/providers/anthropic/response_adapter.rb:26-36`
- Google ties components at `lib/llm/providers/google.rb:20-33`, response adapter registry at `lib/llm/providers/google/response_adapter.rb:28-40`
- Bedrock additionally has a custom `stream_decoder` tied at `lib/llm/providers/bedrock.rb:200-202`

## Step 5: Streaming and error-handling paths

Streaming:

- `execute` wraps a stream target with parser/decoder: `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:31-32`
- Default SSE decoder: `lib/llm/transport/stream_decoder.rb:30-86`
- Provider-specific SSE parsers:
  - OpenAI: `lib/llm/providers/openai/stream_parser.rb`
  - Anthropic: `lib/llm/providers/anthropic/stream_parser.rb`
  - Google: `lib/llm/providers/google/stream_parser.rb`
  - Ollama: `lib/llm/providers/ollama/stream_parser.rb`
- Bedrock uses AWS Event Stream binary framing:
  - Parser: `lib/llm/providers/bedrock/stream_parser.rb`
  - Decoder: `lib/llm/providers/bedrock/stream_decoder.rb`
- OpenAI Responses API has its own parser: `lib/llm/providers/openai/responses/stream_parser.rb`

Error handling:

- `handle_response` dispatches to provider error handler: `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:52`
- Shared error types: `lib/llm/error.rb`
  - `LLM::Error` base → `6`
  - `LLM::UnauthorizedError` → `28`
  - `LLM::RateLimitError` → `32`
  - `LLM::ServerError` → `36`
  - `LLM::NotFoundError` → `40`
  - `LLM::FormatError`/`PromptError` → `44-48`
  - `LLM::InvalidRequestError`/`ContextWindowError` → `52-56`
  - `LLM::Interrupt` → `68`
  - `LLM::NoSuchModelError`/`NoSuchRegistryError` → `80-84`
- Provider error handlers map HTTP status + body to shared types:
  - OpenAI: `lib/llm/providers/openai/error_handler.rb:51-82`
  - Anthropic: `lib/llm/providers/anthropic/error_handler.rb:45-57`
  - Google: `lib/llm/providers/google/error_handler.rb:51-68`
  - Ollama: `lib/llm/providers/ollama/error_handler.rb:45-57`
  - Bedrock: `lib/llm/providers/bedrock/error_handler.rb:45-60`

## Step 6: Sites that must change to add a new provider

1. Factory entry: add `LLM.<newprovider>` method in `lib/llm.rb:112-181`
2. Provider class: create `lib/llm/providers/<newprovider>.rb`
3. Provider components under `lib/llm/providers/<newprovider>/`:
   - `error_handler.rb`
   - `request_adapter.rb`
   - `request_adapter/completion.rb`
   - `response_adapter.rb`
   - `response_adapter/completion.rb`
   - `stream_parser.rb`
   - Optional: `models.rb`, `files.rb`, `images.rb`, `audio.rb`, `utils.rb`, extra response adapters
4. Model registry data: create `data/<newprovider>.json`
5. Registry spec: add context in `spec/registry_spec.rb`
6. Provider specs: create `spec/<newprovider>/chat_spec.rb` (and optional `embedding_spec.rb`, `models_spec.rb`, etc.)
7. VCR cassettes: create `spec/fixtures/cassettes/<newprovider>/`
8. VCR secret filter: add env var filter in `spec/setup.rb:28-36`
9. Rakefile: add to `remotes` list at `Rakefile:7` if remote; add to console task at `Rakefile:49-53`
10. README: add provider example if documenting

If inheriting from OpenAI, reuse `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb` via `require_relative "openai"` as done in `lib/llm/providers/deepseek.rb:3`, `lib/llm/providers/xai.rb:3`, `lib/llm/providers/zai.rb:3`.

## Step 7: Complete edit map

### Files to create

- `spec/fixtures/cassettes/<newprovider>/chat/llm_chat_stream_stringio.yml`
- `spec/fixtures/cassettes/<newprovider>/chat/llm_chat_stream_tool.yml`

### Files to change

- `spec/registry_spec.rb` — add registry context
- `spec/setup.rb:28-36` — add VCR secret filter
- `Rakefile:7` — add to `remotes` if remote

### Optional files to create/change

- `lib/llm/providers/<newprovider>/files.rb` + `response_adapter/file.rb`
